content.content:
1→/**
     2→ * Tests for StarknetService — effect-services category.
     3→ *
     4→ * Strategy: StarknetService is tested via Layer.succeed mocks so no live
     5→ * RPC connection is needed. This covers the contract (method signatures,
     6→ * error types, Exit values) without hitting mainnet.
     7→ *
     8→ * Additionally, the real StarknetService is tested for computeDeployAddress
     9→ * (pure computation — no network call) using ConfigProvider to inject the
    10→ * RPC URL.
    11→ *
    12→ * Coverage requirements:
    13→ * - Every service method must have at least one success and one failure test
    14→ * - StarknetRpcError, AccountDeployError must be producible by the service
    15→ */
    16→
    17→import { describe, it, expect } from "bun:test"
    18→import { Effect, Exit, Layer, ConfigProvider } from "effect"
    19→import { StarknetService } from "../../services/StarknetService"
    20→import { StarknetRpcError, AccountDeployError } from "../../services/errors"
    21→import type { PackedPublicKey, TxHash, ContractAddress } from "../../services/types"
    22→
    23→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    24→// Mock StarknetService helpers
    25→// Use `as any` for mock service objects because test mocks intentionally
    26→// include error-producing methods that differ from the inferred success-only types.
    27→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    28→
    29→const mockAddress = "0x049d36570d4e46f48e99674bd3fcc84644ddd6b96f7c741b1562b82f9e004dc7" as ContractAddress
    30→const mockTxHash = "0x7f3e2a1b9c4d5e6f" as TxHash
    31→
    32→// Success mock: all methods return successful Effects
    33→// eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
    34→const makeSuccessService = (): Layer.Layer<StarknetService> =>
    35→  Layer.succeed(StarknetService, {
    36→    computeDeployAddress: (_pk: PackedPublicKey) =>
    37→      Effect.succeed(mockAddress),
    38→    getBalance: (_addr: string) => Effect.succeed(BigInt("1000000000000000000")),
    39→    deployAccount: (_pk: PackedPublicKey, _sk: string) =>
    40→      Effect.succeed({ txHash: mockTxHash, address: mockAddress }),
    41→    waitForTx: (_tx: string) => Effect.succeed(undefined as void),
    42→    // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
    43→    provider: {} as any,
    44→  // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
    45→  } as any)
    46→
    47→// Failing mock: all methods return failing Effects with typed errors
    48→// eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
    49→const makeFailingService = (): Layer.Layer<StarknetService> =>
    50→  Layer.succeed(StarknetService, {
    51→    computeDeployAddress: (_pk: PackedPublicKey) =>
    52→      Effect.fail(new StarknetRpcError({ message: "mock rpc error", code: -1 })),
    53→    getBalance: (_addr: string) =>
    54→      Effect.fail(new StarknetRpcError({ message: "balance fetch failed", code: -32000 })),
    55→    deployAccount: (_pk: PackedPublicKey, _sk: string) =>
    56→      Effect.fail(new AccountDeployError({ message: "deploy rejected" })),
    57→    waitForTx: (_tx: string) =>
    58→      Effect.fail(new StarknetRpcError({ message: "tx timeout", code: -1 })),
    59→    // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
    60→    provider: {} as any,
    61→  // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
    62→  } as any)
    63→
    64→/** Run an effect provided with a specific StarknetService mock layer */
    65→async function runWith<A, E>(
    66→  effect: Effect.Effect<A, E, StarknetService>,
    67→  serviceLayer: Layer.Layer<StarknetService>,
    68→): Promise<Exit.Exit<A, E>> {
    69→  return Effect.runPromiseExit(
    70→    effect.pipe(Effect.provide(serviceLayer)),
    71→  )
    72→}
    73→
    74→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    75→// computeDeployAddress
    76→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    77→
    78→describe("StarknetService.computeDeployAddress (mocked)", () => {
    79→  const mockPk: PackedPublicKey = {
    80→    slots: Array.from({ length: 29 }, (_, i) => `0x${i.toString(16).padStart(64, "0")}`),
    81→  }
    82→
    83→  it("succeeds and returns a ContractAddress string", async () => {
    84→    const exit = await runWith(
    85→      StarknetService.computeDeployAddress(mockPk),
    86→      makeSuccessService(),
    87→    )
    88→    expect(Exit.isSuccess(exit)).toBe(true)
    89→    if (Exit.isSuccess(exit)) {
    90→      expect(typeof exit.value).toBe("string")
    91→      expect(exit.value.startsWith("0x")).toBe(true)
    92→    }
    93→  })
    94→
    95→  it("fails with StarknetRpcError when service fails", async () => {
    96→    const exit = await runWith(
    97→      StarknetService.computeDeployAddress(mockPk),
    98→      makeFailingService(),
    99→    )
   100→    expect(Exit.isFailure(exit)).toBe(true)
   101→    if (Exit.isFailure(exit)) {
   102→      expect(JSON.stringify(exit.cause)).toContain("StarknetRpcError")
   103→    }
   104→  })
   105→})
   106→
   107→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   108→// getBalance
   109→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   110→
   111→describe("StarknetService.getBalance (mocked)", () => {
   112→  it("succeeds and returns a bigint balance", async () => {
   113→    const exit = await runWith(
   114→      StarknetService.getBalance("0x1234"),
   115→      makeSuccessService(),
   116→    )
   117→    expect(Exit.isSuccess(exit)).toBe(true)
   118→    if (Exit.isSuccess(exit)) {
   119→      expect(typeof exit.value).toBe("bigint")
   120→      expect(exit.value).toBe(BigInt("1000000000000000000"))
   121→    }
   122→  })
   123→
   124→  it("fails with StarknetRpcError when RPC call fails", async () => {
   125→    const exit = await runWith(
   126→      StarknetService.getBalance("0xdeadbeef"),
   127→      makeFailingService(),
   128→    )
   129→    expect(Exit.isFailure(exit)).toBe(true)
   130→    if (Exit.isFailure(exit)) {
   131→      expect(JSON.stringify(exit.cause)).toContain("StarknetRpcError")
   132→    }
   133→  })
   134→})
   135→
   136→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   137→// deployAccount
   138→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   139→
   140→describe("StarknetService.deployAccount (mocked)", () => {
   141→  const mockPk: PackedPublicKey = {
   142→    slots: Array.from({ length: 29 }, (_, i) => `0x${i.toString(16).padStart(64, "0")}`),
   143→  }
   144→
   145→  it("succeeds and returns txHash and address", async () => {
   146→    const exit = await runWith(
   147→      StarknetService.deployAccount(mockPk, "0xprivkey"),
   148→      makeSuccessService(),
   149→    )
   150→    expect(Exit.isSuccess(exit)).toBe(true)
   151→    if (Exit.isSuccess(exit)) {
   152→      expect(exit.value.txHash).toBe(mockTxHash)
   153→      expect(exit.value.address).toBe(mockAddress)
   154→    }
   155→  })
   156→
   157→  it("fails with AccountDeployError when deployment is rejected", async () => {
   158→    const exit = await runWith(
   159→      StarknetService.deployAccount(mockPk, "0xprivkey"),
   160→      makeFailingService(),
   161→    )
   162→    expect(Exit.isFailure(exit)).toBe(true)
   163→    if (Exit.isFailure(exit)) {
   164→      expect(JSON.stringify(exit.cause)).toContain("AccountDeployError")
   165→    }
   166→  })
   167→})
   168→
   169→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   170→// waitForTx
   171→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   172→
   173→describe("StarknetService.waitForTx (mocked)", () => {
   174→  it("succeeds (returns void) when transaction confirms", async () => {
   175→    const exit = await runWith(
   176→      StarknetService.waitForTx("0xabc123"),
   177→      makeSuccessService(),
   178→    )
   179→    expect(Exit.isSuccess(exit)).toBe(true)
   180→  })
   181→
   182→  it("fails with StarknetRpcError when tx times out", async () => {
   183→    const exit = await runWith(
   184→      StarknetService.waitForTx("0xdeadbeef"),
   185→      makeFailingService(),
   186→    )
   187→    expect(Exit.isFailure(exit)).toBe(true)
   188→    if (Exit.isFailure(exit)) {
   189→      expect(JSON.stringify(exit.cause)).toContain("StarknetRpcError")
   190→    }
   191→  })
   192→})
   193→
   194→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   195→// Real StarknetService: computeDeployAddress (pure computation, no network)
   196→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   197→
   198→describe("StarknetService.computeDeployAddress (real implementation)", () => {
   199→  const mockPk: PackedPublicKey = {
   200→    slots: Array.from({ length: 29 }, (_, i) => `0x${i.toString(16).padStart(64, "0")}`),
   201→  }
   202→
   203→  const withTestConfig = ConfigProvider.fromMap(
   204→    new Map([["NEXT_PUBLIC_STARKNET_RPC_URL", "http://localhost:9999"]]),
   205→  )
   206→
   207→  it("returns a hex string address (pure computation, no RPC call)", async () => {
   208→    const exit = await Effect.runPromiseExit(
   209→      StarknetService.computeDeployAddress(mockPk).pipe(
   210→        Effect.provide(StarknetService.Default),
   211→        Effect.provide(Layer.setConfigProvider(withTestConfig)),
   212→      ),
   213→    )
   214→    // computeDeployAddress is pure (hash + CallData only), should succeed
   215→    // even with a fake RPC URL because no network call is made
   216→    expect(Exit.isSuccess(exit)).toBe(true)
   217→    if (Exit.isSuccess(exit)) {
   218→      expect(typeof exit.value).toBe("string")
   219→    }
   220→  })
   221→})
   222→
   223→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   224→// StarknetService: fails when Config is missing
   225→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   226→
   227→describe("StarknetService: initialization failure", () => {
   228→  it("StarknetService.Default fails gracefully when Config is missing", async () => {
   229→    const exit = await Effect.runPromiseExit(
   230→      StarknetService.computeDeployAddress({
   231→        slots: Array.from({ length: 29 }, () => "0x0"),
   232→      }).pipe(
   233→        Effect.provide(StarknetService.Default),
   234→        // Provide an empty ConfigProvider to prevent bun from loading .env.local —
   235→        // this simulates the "no config available" scenario the test describes
   236→        Effect.provide(Layer.setConfigProvider(ConfigProvider.fromMap(new Map()))),
   237→      ),
   238→    )
   239→    // Should fail because NEXT_PUBLIC_STARKNET_RPC_URL is not in the empty config
   240→    expect(Exit.isFailure(exit)).toBe(true)
   241→  })
   242→})
   243→

1→/**
     2→ * Tests for Starknet-specific Schema.TaggedError classes.
     3→ *
     4→ * Coverage requirements:
     5→ * - Every Schema.TaggedError must be constructable and matchable
     6→ * - _tag must equal the class name string
     7→ * - Errors must roundtrip through Effect failure/exit
     8→ */
     9→
    10→import { describe, it, expect } from "bun:test"
    11→import { Effect, Exit, Cause } from "effect"
    12→import {
    13→  StarknetRpcError,
    14→  AccountDeployError,
    15→  InsufficientFundsError,
    16→} from "../../services/errors"
    17→
    18→describe("StarknetRpcError", () => {
    19→  it("is constructable with message and code", () => {
    20→    const err = new StarknetRpcError({
    21→      message: "RPC call failed",
    22→      code: -32000,
    23→    })
    24→    expect(err.message).toBe("RPC call failed")
    25→    expect(err.code).toBe(-32000)
    26→    expect(err._tag).toBe("StarknetRpcError")
    27→  })
    28→
    29→  it("roundtrips through Effect.runPromiseExit", async () => {
    30→    const exit = await Effect.runPromiseExit(
    31→      Effect.fail(new StarknetRpcError({ message: "connection refused", code: -1 })),
    32→    )
    33→    expect(Exit.isFailure(exit)).toBe(true)
    34→    expect(Exit.isSuccess(exit)).toBe(false)
    35→    if (Exit.isFailure(exit)) {
    36→      const err = Cause.failureOption(exit.cause)
    37→      expect(err._tag).toBe("Some")
    38→      // @ts-expect-error — Option.Some wraps the error
    39→      expect(err.value._tag).toBe("StarknetRpcError")
    40→      // @ts-expect-error
    41→      expect(err.value.code).toBe(-1)
    42→    }
    43→  })
    44→
    45→  it("can be caught with catchTag", async () => {
    46→    let caughtCode = 0
    47→    await Effect.runPromise(
    48→      Effect.fail(new StarknetRpcError({ message: "bad request", code: 400 })).pipe(
    49→        Effect.catchTag("StarknetRpcError", (e) => {
    50→          caughtCode = e.code
    51→          return Effect.succeed(null)
    52→        }),
    53→      ),
    54→    )
    55→    expect(caughtCode).toBe(400)
    56→  })
    57→
    58→  it("_tag is 'StarknetRpcError' (not 'Error' or undefined)", () => {
    59→    const err = new StarknetRpcError({ message: "x", code: 0 })
    60→    expect(err._tag).toBe("StarknetRpcError")
    61→    expect(err._tag).not.toBe("Error")
    62→  })
    63→
    64→  it("preserves code=0 (zero is valid, not falsy-filtered)", () => {
    65→    const err = new StarknetRpcError({ message: "ok", code: 0 })
    66→    expect(err.code).toBe(0)
    67→  })
    68→})
    69→
    70→describe("AccountDeployError", () => {
    71→  it("is constructable with message only (txHash optional)", () => {
    72→    const err = new AccountDeployError({ message: "deploy failed" })
    73→    expect(err.message).toBe("deploy failed")
    74→    expect(err._tag).toBe("AccountDeployError")
    75→    expect(err.txHash).toBeUndefined()
    76→  })
    77→
    78→  it("is constructable with message and txHash", () => {
    79→    const err = new AccountDeployError({
    80→      message: "tx reverted",
    81→      txHash: "0xcafebeef",
    82→    })
    83→    expect(err.message).toBe("tx reverted")
    84→    expect(err.txHash).toBe("0xcafebeef")
    85→    expect(err._tag).toBe("AccountDeployError")
    86→  })
    87→
    88→  it("roundtrips through Effect.runPromiseExit", async () => {
    89→    const exit = await Effect.runPromiseExit(
    90→      Effect.fail(
    91→        new AccountDeployError({ message: "nonce too low", txHash: "0xabc" }),
    92→      ),
    93→    )
    94→    expect(Exit.isFailure(exit)).toBe(true)
    95→    if (Exit.isFailure(exit)) {
    96→      const err = Cause.failureOption(exit.cause)
    97→      // @ts-expect-error
    98→      expect(err.value._tag).toBe("AccountDeployError")
    99→      // @ts-expect-error
   100→      expect(err.value.txHash).toBe("0xabc")
   101→    }
   102→  })
   103→
   104→  it("can be caught with catchTag and txHash is accessible", async () => {
   105→    let caughtHash: string | undefined = undefined
   106→    await Effect.runPromise(
   107→      Effect.fail(
   108→        new AccountDeployError({ message: "failed", txHash: "0xdeadbeef" }),
   109→      ).pipe(
   110→        Effect.catchTag("AccountDeployError", (e) => {
   111→          caughtHash = e.txHash
   112→          return Effect.succeed(null)
   113→        }),
   114→      ),
   115→    )
   116→    expect(caughtHash!).toBe("0xdeadbeef")
   117→  })
   118→
   119→  it("succeeds in an effect chain when no error is thrown", async () => {
   120→    const exit = await Effect.runPromiseExit(
   121→      Effect.succeed({ txHash: "0x123", address: "0xabc" }),
   122→    )
   123→    expect(Exit.isSuccess(exit)).toBe(true)
   124→  })
   125→})
   126→
   127→describe("InsufficientFundsError", () => {
   128→  it("is constructable with message, address, and required", () => {
   129→    const err = new InsufficientFundsError({
   130→      message: "need more ETH",
   131→      address: "0x1234",
   132→      required: "1000000000000000000",
   133→    })
   134→    expect(err.message).toBe("need more ETH")
   135→    expect(err.address).toBe("0x1234")
   136→    expect(err.required).toBe("1000000000000000000")
   137→    expect(err._tag).toBe("InsufficientFundsError")
   138→  })
   139→
   140→  it("roundtrips through Effect.runPromiseExit", async () => {
   141→    const exit = await Effect.runPromiseExit(
   142→      Effect.fail(
   143→        new InsufficientFundsError({
   144→          message: "balance too low",
   145→          address: "0xbeef",
   146→          required: "500",
   147→        }),
   148→      ),
   149→    )
   150→    expect(Exit.isFailure(exit)).toBe(true)
   151→    if (Exit.isFailure(exit)) {
   152→      const err = Cause.failureOption(exit.cause)
   153→      // @ts-expect-error
   154→      expect(err.value._tag).toBe("InsufficientFundsError")
   155→      // @ts-expect-error
   156→      expect(err.value.address).toBe("0xbeef")
   157→    }
   158→  })
   159→
   160→  it("can be caught with catchTag and address is accessible", async () => {
   161→    let caughtAddress = ""
   162→    await Effect.runPromise(
   163→      Effect.fail(
   164→        new InsufficientFundsError({
   165→          message: "not enough",
   166→          address: "0xfeed",
   167→          required: "999",
   168→        }),
   169→      ).pipe(
   170→        Effect.catchTag("InsufficientFundsError", (e) => {
   171→          caughtAddress = e.address
   172→          return Effect.succeed(null)
   173→        }),
   174→      ),
   175→    )
   176→    expect(caughtAddress).toBe("0xfeed")
   177→  })
   178→
   179→  it("is distinct from StarknetRpcError (different _tag)", () => {
   180→    const rpcErr = new StarknetRpcError({ message: "x", code: 0 })
   181→    const fundsErr = new InsufficientFundsError({
   182→      message: "y",
   183→      address: "0x0",
   184→      required: "0",
   185→    })
   186→    expect(rpcErr._tag).not.toBe(fundsErr._tag)
   187→    expect(fundsErr._tag).toBe("InsufficientFundsError")
   188→  })
   189→})
   190→

1→/**
     2→ * Integration tests for Pipeline Visualizer (pipeline-viz)
     3→ *
     4→ * Tests the atoms and state machine logic for the 6-step animated walkthrough.
     5→ * These tests cover:
     6→ *   - INITIAL_PIPELINE_STEPS data correctness (6 steps, correct counts)
     7→ *   - Atom initial values (pipelineStepsAtom, pipelineActiveStepAtom, pipelinePlayingAtom)
     8→ *   - State transitions via Registry (play → step through → reset)
     9→ *
    10→ * The PipelineVisualizer.tsx component is NOT YET IMPLEMENTED.
    11→ * These tests verify the data and atom layer that the component will depend on.
    12→ *
    13→ * Test runner: bun test
    14→ * Dependency: @effect-atom/atom Registry API for read/write without React
    15→ */
    16→
    17→import { describe, it, expect, beforeEach } from "bun:test"
    18→import { Registry } from "@effect-atom/atom"
    19→import {
    20→  INITIAL_PIPELINE_STEPS,
    21→  pipelineStepsAtom,
    22→  pipelineActiveStepAtom,
    23→  pipelinePlayingAtom,
    24→} from "../atoms/pipeline"
    25→import type { PipelineStep } from "../services/types"
    26→
    27→// ─── Helpers ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    28→
    29→/** Simulate the updateStepStatuses logic from PipelineVisualizer.tsx */
    30→function computeStepStatuses(
    31→  steps: PipelineStep[],
    32→  activeIdx: number,
    33→): PipelineStep[] {
    34→  return steps.map((s, i) => ({
    35→    ...s,
    36→    status:
    37→      i < activeIdx
    38→        ? "complete"
    39→        : i === activeIdx
    40→          ? "active"
    41→          : "pending",
    42→  }))
    43→}
    44→
    45→/** Create a fresh Registry for each test (isolated state) */
    46→function makeRegistry() {
    47→  return Registry.make({
    48→    initialValues: [
    49→      [pipelineStepsAtom, INITIAL_PIPELINE_STEPS],
    50→      [pipelineActiveStepAtom, -1],
    51→      [pipelinePlayingAtom, false],
    52→    ],
    53→  })
    54→}
    55→
    56→// ─── Tests ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    57→
    58→describe("INITIAL_PIPELINE_STEPS data", () => {
    59→  it("has exactly 6 pipeline steps", () => {
    60→    expect(INITIAL_PIPELINE_STEPS).toHaveLength(6)
    61→  })
    62→
    63→  it("all steps start with status pending", () => {
    64→    for (const step of INITIAL_PIPELINE_STEPS) {
    65→      expect(step.status).toBe("pending")
    66→    }
    67→  })
    68→
    69→  it("step IDs are unique", () => {
    70→    const ids = INITIAL_PIPELINE_STEPS.map((s) => s.id)
    71→    const uniqueIds = new Set(ids)
    72→    expect(uniqueIds.size).toBe(6)
    73→  })
    74→
    75→  it("step counts match PRD specification", () => {
    76→    // From docs/specs/falcon-demo-website.md
    77→    const expected = [5988, 15000, 1500, 15000, 500, 26000]
    78→    const actual = INITIAL_PIPELINE_STEPS.map((s) => s.stepCount)
    79→    expect(actual).toEqual(expected)
    80→  })
    81→
    82→  it("total step count is ~63988 (matches README ~63K)", () => {
    83→    const total = INITIAL_PIPELINE_STEPS.reduce((sum, s) => sum + s.stepCount, 0)
    84→    // PRD says ~63K steps total for verify
    85→    expect(total).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(60000)
    86→    expect(total).toBeLessThanOrEqual(70000)
    87→  })
    88→
    89→  it("step names match specification", () => {
    90→    const names = INITIAL_PIPELINE_STEPS.map((s) => s.name)
    91→    expect(names[0]).toBe("hash_to_point")
    92→    expect(names[1]).toBe("NTT(s1)")
    93→    expect(names[2]).toBe("s1_ntt * pk_ntt")
    94→    expect(names[3]).toBe("NTT(mul_hint)")
    95→    expect(names[4]).toBe("s0 = msg_point - mul_hint")
    96→    expect(names[5]).toContain("‖")  // norm check uses ‖ symbol
    97→  })
    98→
    99→  it("step IDs match expected values", () => {
   100→    const ids = INITIAL_PIPELINE_STEPS.map((s) => s.id)
   101→    expect(ids[0]).toBe("hash-to-point")
   102→    expect(ids[1]).toBe("ntt-s1")
   103→    expect(ids[2]).toBe("pointwise-mul")
   104→    expect(ids[3]).toBe("ntt-hint")
   105→    expect(ids[4]).toBe("recover-s0")
   106→    expect(ids[5]).toBe("norm-check")
   107→  })
   108→
   109→  it("all steps have non-empty input/output/description fields", () => {
   110→    for (const step of INITIAL_PIPELINE_STEPS) {
   111→      expect(step.input).toBeTruthy()
   112→      expect(step.output).toBeTruthy()
   113→      expect(step.description).toBeTruthy()
   114→    }
   115→  })
   116→})
   117→
   118→describe("pipeline atoms — initial state", () => {
   119→  let registry: Registry.Registry
   120→
   121→  beforeEach(() => {
   122→    registry = makeRegistry()
   123→  })
   124→
   125→  it("pipelineStepsAtom initial value has 6 steps", () => {
   126→    const steps = registry.get(pipelineStepsAtom)
   127→    expect(steps).toHaveLength(6)
   128→  })
   129→
   130→  it("pipelineActiveStepAtom initial value is -1 (no active step)", () => {
   131→    const activeStep = registry.get(pipelineActiveStepAtom)
   132→    expect(activeStep).toBe(-1)
   133→  })
   134→
   135→  it("pipelinePlayingAtom initial value is false (not playing)", () => {
   136→    const playing = registry.get(pipelinePlayingAtom)
   137→    expect(playing).toBe(false)
   138→  })
   139→
   140→  it("all pipeline steps start as pending", () => {
   141→    const steps = registry.get(pipelineStepsAtom)
   142→    for (const step of steps) {
   143→      expect(step.status).toBe("pending")
   144→    }
   145→  })
   146→})
   147→
   148→describe("pipeline atoms — play/step state transitions", () => {
   149→  let registry: Registry.Registry
   150→
   151→  beforeEach(() => {
   152→    registry = makeRegistry()
   153→  })
   154→
   155→  it("setPlaying(true) updates pipelinePlayingAtom", () => {
   156→    registry.set(pipelinePlayingAtom, true)
   157→    expect(registry.get(pipelinePlayingAtom)).toBe(true)
   158→  })
   159→
   160→  it("step 0 becomes active when activeStep is set to 0", () => {
   161→    registry.set(pipelineActiveStepAtom, 0)
   162→    const activeStep = registry.get(pipelineActiveStepAtom)
   163→    expect(activeStep).toBe(0)
   164→
   165→    // Update step statuses (simulating PipelineVisualizer.updateStepStatuses(0))
   166→    const steps = registry.get(pipelineStepsAtom)
   167→    const updated = computeStepStatuses(steps, 0)
   168→    registry.set(pipelineStepsAtom, updated)
   169→
   170→    const updatedSteps = registry.get(pipelineStepsAtom)
   171→    expect(updatedSteps[0].status).toBe("active")
   172→    expect(updatedSteps[1].status).toBe("pending")
   173→    expect(updatedSteps[5].status).toBe("pending")
   174→  })
   175→
   176→  it("after advancing to step 3, steps 0-2 are complete, step 3 is active, rest pending", () => {
   177→    registry.set(pipelineActiveStepAtom, 3)
   178→    const steps = registry.get(pipelineStepsAtom)
   179→    const updated = computeStepStatuses(steps, 3)
   180→    registry.set(pipelineStepsAtom, updated)
   181→
   182→    const updatedSteps = registry.get(pipelineStepsAtom)
   183→    expect(updatedSteps[0].status).toBe("complete")
   184→    expect(updatedSteps[1].status).toBe("complete")
   185→    expect(updatedSteps[2].status).toBe("complete")
   186→    expect(updatedSteps[3].status).toBe("active")
   187→    expect(updatedSteps[4].status).toBe("pending")
   188→    expect(updatedSteps[5].status).toBe("pending")
   189→  })
   190→
   191→  it("at the last step (5), steps 0-4 are complete, step 5 is active", () => {
   192→    registry.set(pipelineActiveStepAtom, 5)
   193→    const steps = registry.get(pipelineStepsAtom)
   194→    const updated = computeStepStatuses(steps, 5)
   195→    registry.set(pipelineStepsAtom, updated)
   196→
   197→    const updatedSteps = registry.get(pipelineStepsAtom)
   198→    for (let i = 0; i < 5; i++) {
   199→      expect(updatedSteps[i].status).toBe("complete")
   200→    }
   201→    expect(updatedSteps[5].status).toBe("active")
   202→  })
   203→
   204→  it("reset: all steps return to pending, activeStep to -1, playing to false", () => {
   205→    // First advance to step 3
   206→    registry.set(pipelineActiveStepAtom, 3)
   207→    registry.set(pipelinePlayingAtom, true)
   208→    const steps = registry.get(pipelineStepsAtom)
   209→    registry.set(pipelineStepsAtom, computeStepStatuses(steps, 3))
   210→
   211→    // Now reset
   212→    registry.set(pipelinePlayingAtom, false)
   213→    registry.set(pipelineActiveStepAtom, -1)
   214→    const resetSteps = registry.get(pipelineStepsAtom)
   215→    registry.set(
   216→      pipelineStepsAtom,
   217→      resetSteps.map((s) => ({ ...s, status: "pending" as const })),
   218→    )
   219→
   220→    // Verify reset state
   221→    expect(registry.get(pipelinePlayingAtom)).toBe(false)
   222→    expect(registry.get(pipelineActiveStepAtom)).toBe(-1)
   223→    const finalSteps = registry.get(pipelineStepsAtom)
   224→    for (const step of finalSteps) {
   225→      expect(step.status).toBe("pending")
   226→    }
   227→  })
   228→
   229→  it("step atom can be advanced one step at a time", () => {
   230→    // Simulate: no active step → step 0 → step 1 → step 2
   231→    for (let i = 0; i <= 2; i++) {
   232→      registry.set(pipelineActiveStepAtom, i)
   233→      const steps = registry.get(pipelineStepsAtom)
   234→      registry.set(pipelineStepsAtom, computeStepStatuses(steps, i))
   235→    }
   236→
   237→    const finalSteps = registry.get(pipelineStepsAtom)
   238→    expect(registry.get(pipelineActiveStepAtom)).toBe(2)
   239→    expect(finalSteps[0].status).toBe("complete")
   240→    expect(finalSteps[1].status).toBe("complete")
   241→    expect(finalSteps[2].status).toBe("active")
   242→    expect(finalSteps[3].status).toBe("pending")
   243→  })
   244→
   245→  it("step immutability: updating steps does not mutate INITIAL_PIPELINE_STEPS", () => {
   246→    const steps = registry.get(pipelineStepsAtom)
   247→    const updated = computeStepStatuses(steps, 2)
   248→    registry.set(pipelineStepsAtom, updated)
   249→
   250→    // Original constant should be unchanged
   251→    for (const step of INITIAL_PIPELINE_STEPS) {
   252→      expect(step.status).toBe("pending")
   253→    }
   254→  })
   255→})
   256→
   257→describe("computeStepStatuses helper — state machine logic", () => {
   258→  const steps = INITIAL_PIPELINE_STEPS
   259→
   260→  it("at index -1: all steps remain pending", () => {
   261→    const result = computeStepStatuses(steps, -1)
   262→    // When activeIdx is -1, no step satisfies i === -1, so all stay "pending"
   263→    // (i < -1 is never true for non-negative i)
   264→    for (const s of result) {
   265→      expect(s.status).toBe("pending")
   266→    }
   267→  })
   268→
   269→  it("at index 0: first step is active, rest pending", () => {
   270→    const result = computeStepStatuses(steps, 0)
   271→    expect(result[0].status).toBe("active")
   272→    for (let i = 1; i < result.length; i++) {
   273→      expect(result[i].status).toBe("pending")
   274→    }
   275→  })
   276→
   277→  it("at index 2: first two complete, third active, rest pending", () => {
   278→    const result = computeStepStatuses(steps, 2)
   279→    expect(result[0].status).toBe("complete")
   280→    expect(result[1].status).toBe("complete")
   281→    expect(result[2].status).toBe("active")
   282→    expect(result[3].status).toBe("pending")
   283→    expect(result[4].status).toBe("pending")
   284→    expect(result[5].status).toBe("pending")
   285→  })
   286→
   287→  it("at index 5 (last): all five previous complete, last active", () => {
   288→    const result = computeStepStatuses(steps, 5)
   289→    for (let i = 0; i < 5; i++) {
   290→      expect(result[i].status).toBe("complete")
   291→    }
   292→    expect(result[5].status).toBe("active")
   293→  })
   294→
   295→  it("preserves step id, name, description, stepCount (immutable fields)", () => {
   296→    const result = computeStepStatuses(steps, 1)
   297→    for (let i = 0; i < result.length; i++) {
   298→      expect(result[i].id).toBe(steps[i].id)
   299→      expect(result[i].name).toBe(steps[i].name)
   300→      expect(result[i].description).toBe(steps[i].description)
   301→      expect(result[i].stepCount).toBe(steps[i].stepCount)
   302→    }
   303→  })
   304→})
   305→

1→/**
     2→ * Unit tests for pipeline.ts atoms.
     3→ *
     4→ * Tests cover:
     5→ * - Default states of pipelineStepsAtom (6 steps, all pending)
     6→ * - Exact step counts matching README profiling metrics
     7→ * - Default state of pipelineActiveStepAtom (-1)
     8→ * - Default state of pipelinePlayingAtom (false)
     9→ * - keepAlive = false on all pipeline atoms
    10→ */
    11→
    12→import { describe, it, expect } from "bun:test"
    13→import { Registry } from "@effect-atom/atom"
    14→
    15→import {
    16→  pipelineStepsAtom,
    17→  pipelineActiveStepAtom,
    18→  pipelinePlayingAtom,
    19→  INITIAL_PIPELINE_STEPS,
    20→} from "../../src/atoms/pipeline"
    21→
    22→function makeRegistry() {
    23→  return Registry.make()
    24→}
    25→
    26→// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    27→// keepAlive properties
    28→// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    29→
    30→describe("pipeline atom.keepAlive properties", () => {
    31→  it("pipelineStepsAtom has keepAlive = false (default)", () => {
    32→    expect(pipelineStepsAtom.keepAlive).toBe(false)
    33→  })
    34→
    35→  it("pipelineActiveStepAtom has keepAlive = false (default)", () => {
    36→    expect(pipelineActiveStepAtom.keepAlive).toBe(false)
    37→  })
    38→
    39→  it("pipelinePlayingAtom has keepAlive = false (default)", () => {
    40→    expect(pipelinePlayingAtom.keepAlive).toBe(false)
    41→  })
    42→})
    43→
    44→// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    45→// pipelineStepsAtom — exact 6 steps with README step counts
    46→// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    47→
    48→describe("pipelineStepsAtom defaults", () => {
    49→  it("has exactly 6 initial pipeline steps", () => {
    50→    const registry = makeRegistry()
    51→    const steps = registry.get(pipelineStepsAtom)
    52→    expect(steps).toHaveLength(6)
    53→  })
    54→
    55→  it("all 6 initial steps have status 'pending'", () => {
    56→    const registry = makeRegistry()
    57→    const steps = registry.get(pipelineStepsAtom)
    58→    expect(steps.every((s) => s.status === "pending")).toBe(true)
    59→  })
    60→
    61→  it("step IDs are correct and in order", () => {
    62→    const registry = makeRegistry()
    63→    const steps = registry.get(pipelineStepsAtom)
    64→    const ids = steps.map((s) => s.id)
    65→    expect(ids).toEqual([
    66→      "hash-to-point",
    67→      "ntt-s1",
    68→      "pointwise-mul",
    69→      "ntt-hint",
    70→      "recover-s0",
    71→      "norm-check",
    72→    ])
    73→  })
    74→
    75→  it("step[0] hash-to-point has stepCount = 5988 (README: hash_to_point profiling)", () => {
    76→    const registry = makeRegistry()
    77→    const steps = registry.get(pipelineStepsAtom)
    78→    expect(steps[0].id).toBe("hash-to-point")
    79→    expect(steps[0].stepCount).toBe(5988)
    80→  })
    81→
    82→  it("step[1] NTT(s1) has stepCount = 15000 (NTT-512 approximate)", () => {
    83→    const registry = makeRegistry()
    84→    const steps = registry.get(pipelineStepsAtom)
    85→    expect(steps[1].id).toBe("ntt-s1")
    86→    expect(steps[1].stepCount).toBe(15000)
    87→  })
    88→
    89→  it("step[2] pointwise-mul has stepCount = 1500", () => {
    90→    const registry = makeRegistry()
    91→    const steps = registry.get(pipelineStepsAtom)
    92→    expect(steps[2].id).toBe("pointwise-mul")
    93→    expect(steps[2].stepCount).toBe(1500)
    94→  })
    95→
    96→  it("step[3] NTT(mul_hint) has stepCount = 15000", () => {
    97→    const registry = makeRegistry()
    98→    const steps = registry.get(pipelineStepsAtom)
    99→    expect(steps[3].id).toBe("ntt-hint")
   100→    expect(steps[3].stepCount).toBe(15000)
   101→  })
   102→
   103→  it("step[4] recover-s0 has stepCount = 500", () => {
   104→    const registry = makeRegistry()
   105→    const steps = registry.get(pipelineStepsAtom)
   106→    expect(steps[4].id).toBe("recover-s0")
   107→    expect(steps[4].stepCount).toBe(500)
   108→  })
   109→
   110→  it("step[5] norm-check has stepCount = 26000 (norm check dominates: ~26K steps)", () => {
   111→    const registry = makeRegistry()
   112→    const steps = registry.get(pipelineStepsAtom)
   113→    expect(steps[5].id).toBe("norm-check")
   114→    expect(steps[5].stepCount).toBe(26000)
   115→  })
   116→
   117→  it("total step count = 63988 (5988+15000+1500+15000+500+26000)", () => {
   118→    const registry = makeRegistry()
   119→    const steps = registry.get(pipelineStepsAtom)
   120→    const total = steps.reduce((sum, s) => sum + s.stepCount, 0)
   121→    expect(total).toBe(63988)
   122→  })
   123→
   124→  it("step counts match the exact spec: [5988, 15000, 1500, 15000, 500, 26000]", () => {
   125→    const registry = makeRegistry()
   126→    const steps = registry.get(pipelineStepsAtom)
   127→    const counts = steps.map((s) => s.stepCount)
   128→    expect(counts).toEqual([5988, 15000, 1500, 15000, 500, 26000])
   129→  })
   130→
   131→  it("can reset all steps to pending via INITIAL_PIPELINE_STEPS", () => {
   132→    const registry = makeRegistry()
   133→    const steps = registry.get(pipelineStepsAtom)
   134→    // advance to complete
   135→    registry.set(pipelineStepsAtom, steps.map((s) => ({ ...s, status: "complete" as const })))
   136→    expect(registry.get(pipelineStepsAtom).every((s) => s.status === "complete")).toBe(true)
   137→    // reset
   138→    registry.set(pipelineStepsAtom, INITIAL_PIPELINE_STEPS)
   139→    expect(registry.get(pipelineStepsAtom).every((s) => s.status === "pending")).toBe(true)
   140→  })
   141→
   142→  it("each step has required fields: id, name, description, input, output, stepCount, status", () => {
   143→    const registry = makeRegistry()
   144→    const steps = registry.get(pipelineStepsAtom)
   145→    for (const step of steps) {
   146→      expect(typeof step.id).toBe("string")
   147→      expect(typeof step.name).toBe("string")
   148→      expect(typeof step.description).toBe("string")
   149→      expect(typeof step.input).toBe("string")
   150→      expect(typeof step.output).toBe("string")
   151→      expect(typeof step.stepCount).toBe("number")
   152→      expect(["pending", "active", "complete"]).toContain(step.status)
   153→    }
   154→  })
   155→})
   156→
   157→// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   158→// pipelineActiveStepAtom — default state
   159→// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   160→
   161→describe("pipelineActiveStepAtom defaults", () => {
   162→  it("has initial value -1 (no active step)", () => {
   163→    const registry = makeRegistry()
   164→    expect(registry.get(pipelineActiveStepAtom)).toBe(-1)
   165→  })
   166→
   167→  it("can be set to any step index 0-5", () => {
   168→    const registry = makeRegistry()
   169→    for (let i = 0; i < 6; i++) {
   170→      registry.set(pipelineActiveStepAtom, i)
   171→      expect(registry.get(pipelineActiveStepAtom)).toBe(i)
   172→    }
   173→  })
   174→
   175→  it("can be reset to -1", () => {
   176→    const registry = makeRegistry()
   177→    registry.set(pipelineActiveStepAtom, 3)
   178→    registry.set(pipelineActiveStepAtom, -1)
   179→    expect(registry.get(pipelineActiveStepAtom)).toBe(-1)
   180→  })
   181→
   182→  it("supports increment via registry.update", () => {
   183→    const registry = makeRegistry()
   184→    registry.set(pipelineActiveStepAtom, 2)
   185→    registry.update(pipelineActiveStepAtom, (prev) => prev + 1)
   186→    expect(registry.get(pipelineActiveStepAtom)).toBe(3)
   187→  })
   188→})
   189→
   190→// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   191→// pipelinePlayingAtom — default state
   192→// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   193→
   194→describe("pipelinePlayingAtom defaults", () => {
   195→  it("has initial value false", () => {
   196→    const registry = makeRegistry()
   197→    expect(registry.get(pipelinePlayingAtom)).toBe(false)
   198→  })
   199→
   200→  it("transitions to true on play", () => {
   201→    const registry = makeRegistry()
   202→    registry.set(pipelinePlayingAtom, true)
   203→    expect(registry.get(pipelinePlayingAtom)).toBe(true)
   204→  })
   205→
   206→  it("transitions to false on pause", () => {
   207→    const registry = makeRegistry()
   208→    registry.set(pipelinePlayingAtom, true)
   209→    registry.set(pipelinePlayingAtom, false)
   210→    expect(registry.get(pipelinePlayingAtom)).toBe(false)
   211→  })
   212→
   213→  it("toggle behavior via registry.update", () => {
   214→    const registry = makeRegistry()
   215→    registry.update(pipelinePlayingAtom, (v) => !v)
   216→    expect(registry.get(pipelinePlayingAtom)).toBe(true)
   217→    registry.update(pipelinePlayingAtom, (v) => !v)
   218→    expect(registry.get(pipelinePlayingAtom)).toBe(false)
   219→  })
   220→})
   221→

1→/**
     2→ * Unit tests for falcon.ts atoms.
     3→ *
     4→ * Tests cover:
     5→ * - Default states of all 5 atoms
     6→ * - keepAlive property on wasmStatusAtom and keypairAtom
     7→ * - State transitions via Registry
     8→ */
     9→
    10→import { describe, it, expect } from "bun:test"
    11→import { Registry } from "@effect-atom/atom"
    12→import { Option } from "effect"
    13→
    14→import {
    15→  wasmStatusAtom,
    16→  keypairAtom,
    17→  signatureAtom,
    18→  verificationStepAtom,
    19→  messageAtom,
    20→} from "../../src/atoms/falcon"
    21→import type { FalconKeypair, FalconSignatureResult, VerificationStep } from "../../src/services/types"
    22→
    23→function makeRegistry() {
    24→  return Registry.make()
    25→}
    26→
    27→// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    28→// keepAlive properties — both must be true
    29→// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    30→
    31→describe("atom.keepAlive properties", () => {
    32→  it("wasmStatusAtom has keepAlive = true (persists without subscribers)", () => {
    33→    expect(wasmStatusAtom.keepAlive).toBe(true)
    34→  })
    35→
    36→  it("keypairAtom has keepAlive = true (persists without subscribers)", () => {
    37→    expect(keypairAtom.keepAlive).toBe(true)
    38→  })
    39→
    40→  it("signatureAtom has keepAlive = false (default, not persistent)", () => {
    41→    expect(signatureAtom.keepAlive).toBe(false)
    42→  })
    43→
    44→  it("verificationStepAtom has keepAlive = false (default, not persistent)", () => {
    45→    expect(verificationStepAtom.keepAlive).toBe(false)
    46→  })
    47→
    48→  it("messageAtom has keepAlive = false (default, not persistent)", () => {
    49→    expect(messageAtom.keepAlive).toBe(false)
    50→  })
    51→})
    52→
    53→// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    54→// wasmStatusAtom — default state
    55→// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    56→
    57→describe("wasmStatusAtom defaults", () => {
    58→  it("has initial value 'loading'", () => {
    59→    const registry = makeRegistry()
    60→    expect(registry.get(wasmStatusAtom)).toBe("loading")
    61→  })
    62→})
    63→
    64→// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    65→// keypairAtom — default state
    66→// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    67→
    68→describe("keypairAtom defaults", () => {
    69→  it("has initial value Option.none()", () => {
    70→    const registry = makeRegistry()
    71→    expect(Option.isNone(registry.get(keypairAtom))).toBe(true)
    72→  })
    73→
    74→  it("accepts a FalconKeypair value wrapped in Option.some()", () => {
    75→    const registry = makeRegistry()
    76→    const mockKeypair: FalconKeypair = {
    77→      secretKey: new Uint8Array(32),
    78→      verifyingKey: new Uint8Array(64),
    79→      publicKeyNtt: new Int32Array(512),
    80→    }
    81→    registry.set(keypairAtom, Option.some(mockKeypair))
    82→    const val = registry.get(keypairAtom)
    83→    expect(Option.isSome(val)).toBe(true)
    84→    if (Option.isSome(val)) {
    85→      expect(val.value.secretKey).toBeInstanceOf(Uint8Array)
    86→      expect(val.value.publicKeyNtt).toBeInstanceOf(Int32Array)
    87→    }
    88→  })
    89→})
    90→
    91→// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    92→// signatureAtom — default state
    93→// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    94→
    95→describe("signatureAtom defaults", () => {
    96→  it("has initial value Option.none()", () => {
    97→    const registry = makeRegistry()
    98→    expect(Option.isNone(registry.get(signatureAtom))).toBe(true)
    99→  })
   100→
   101→  it("accepts a FalconSignatureResult value", () => {
   102→    const registry = makeRegistry()
   103→    const mockSig: FalconSignatureResult = {
   104→      signature: new Uint8Array(666),
   105→      salt: new Uint8Array(40),
   106→    }
   107→    registry.set(signatureAtom, Option.some(mockSig))
   108→    const val = registry.get(signatureAtom)
   109→    expect(Option.isSome(val)).toBe(true)
   110→  })
   111→})
   112→
   113→// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   114→// verificationStepAtom — default state
   115→// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   116→
   117→describe("verificationStepAtom defaults", () => {
   118→  it("has initial value { step: 'idle' }", () => {
   119→    const registry = makeRegistry()
   120→    const val = registry.get(verificationStepAtom)
   121→    expect(val).toEqual({ step: "idle" })
   122→  })
   123→
   124→  it("accepts all valid VerificationStep values", () => {
   125→    const registry = makeRegistry()
   126→    const steps: VerificationStep[] = [
   127→      { step: "generating-keypair" },
   128→      { step: "signing" },
   129→      { step: "creating-hint" },
   130→      { step: "packing" },
   131→      { step: "verifying", substep: "norm-check" },
   132→      { step: "complete", valid: true, durationMs: 500 },
   133→      { step: "error", message: "WASM failed" },
   134→    ]
   135→    for (const step of steps) {
   136→      registry.set(verificationStepAtom, step)
   137→      expect(registry.get(verificationStepAtom)).toEqual(step)
   138→    }
   139→  })
   140→})
   141→
   142→// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   143→// messageAtom — default state
   144→// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   145→
   146→describe("messageAtom defaults", () => {
   147→  it("has initial value empty string ''", () => {
   148→    const registry = makeRegistry()
   149→    expect(registry.get(messageAtom)).toBe("")
   150→  })
   151→
   152→  it("accepts any string value", () => {
   153→    const registry = makeRegistry()
   154→    registry.set(messageAtom, "Hello, Falcon!")
   155→    expect(registry.get(messageAtom)).toBe("Hello, Falcon!")
   156→  })
   157→})
   158→

Now I have a complete picture of the entire demo app. Here is the comprehensive report:

---

## Demo App Architecture — Complete Report

### 1. Directory Structure

```
apps/demo/
├── .env / .env.local           — RPC URL config (identical)
├── next.config.mjs             — WebAssembly async module config
├── tsconfig.json               — Strict TS, bundler resolution, @/* alias
├── package.json                — Next 15, React 19, Effect, starknet, Tailwind
├── findings.md                 — Manual testing report (2026-02-24)
├── public/
│   └── wasm/
│       ├── falcon_rs.js        — WASM JS glue (16.6K, wasm-pack output)
│       └── falcon_rs_bg.wasm   — Falcon-512 WASM binary (254.7K)
├── src/
│   ├── app/
│   │   ├── page.tsx            — Root RSC: composes all sections
│   │   ├── layout.tsx          — HTML shell, metadata, dark class, RegistryProvider
│   │   ├── providers.tsx       — RegistryProvider client wrapper
│   │   └── globals.css         — Tailwind @theme, dark/light tokens
│   ├── atoms/
│   │   ├── falcon.ts           — WASM status, keypair, signature, step, message atoms
│   │   ├── pipeline.ts         — 6-step pipeline data + atoms
│   │   └── starknet.ts         — Deploy step machine + address/txHash atoms
│   ├── components/
│   │   ├── ThemeToggle.tsx     — Theme toggle component (EXISTS but NOT wired into layout)
│   │   ├── landing/
│   │   │   ├── Hero.tsx        — Headline, stat cards (63K/62/29), CTA buttons (RSC)
│   │   │   ├── WhyPostQuantum.tsx — 4 educational cards (RSC)
│   │   │   ├── PerformanceStats.tsx — Benchmark table (RSC)
│   │   │   └── Footer.tsx      — GitHub + Starknet docs links (RSC)
│   │   └── interactive/
│   │       ├── PlaygroundSection.tsx      — dynamic() SSR=false wrapper for VerificationPlayground
│   │       ├── VerificationPlayground.tsx — Keygen + sign + verify client component
│   │       ├── PipelineSection.tsx        — dynamic() SSR=false wrapper for PipelineVisualizer
│   │       ├── PipelineVisualizer.tsx     — 6-step animated walkthrough with play/pause/step/reset
│   │       ├── AccountDeploySection.tsx   — dynamic() SSR=false wrapper for AccountDeployFlow
│   │       ├── AccountDeployFlow.tsx      — 5-step deploy wizard client component
│   │       ├── accountDeployPipeline.ts   — Effect functions for deploy pipeline logic
│   │       ├── HexDisplay.tsx             — Reusable hex/felt string display component
│   │       └── verification-utils.ts      — bytesToHex, truncateHex, getVerificationDisabledState
│   ├── services/
│   │   ├── WasmRuntime.ts      — Context.Tag + WasmRuntimeLive Layer, isValidWasmModule guard
│   │   ├── FalconService.ts    — Effect service: keygen, sign, verify, createHint, packPublicKey
│   │   ├── StarknetService.ts  — Effect service: computeDeployAddress, getBalance, deployAccount
│   │   ├── types.ts            — Domain types: FalconKeypair, VerificationStep, PipelineStep, etc.
│   │   ├── errors.ts           — Schema.TaggedError classes (8 error types)
│   │   └── __tests__/
│   │       └── WasmRuntime.test.ts
│   └── __tests__/
│       ├── pipeline-viz.test.ts
│       ├── account-deploy/
│       │   ├── account-deploy-pipeline.test.ts

```

---

### 2. Package Dependencies

| Category | Package | Version | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|
| Runtime | `next` | ^15.1.0 | App framework (Turbopack dev) |
| Runtime | `react` / `react-dom` | ^19.0.0 | UI |
| Runtime | `effect` | ^3.13.0 | Functional effects, schemas, tagged errors |
| Runtime | `@effect-atom/atom` | ^0.5.0 | Atom state management |
| Runtime | `@effect-atom/atom-react` | ^0.5.0 | React hooks for atoms |
| Runtime | `starknet` | ^9.2.1 | RpcProvider, Account, hash, CallData, stark |
| Runtime | `tailwindcss` | ^4.2.1 | Styling |
| Dev | `typescript` | ^5.7.0 | |
| Dev | `@types/bun` | ^1.3.9 | Bun test runner types |
| Test runner | bun | (system) | `bun test` |

Scripts: `dev` (turbopack), `build`, `start`, `typecheck` (tsc --noEmit), `test` (bun test)

---

### 3. Configuration Files

**`next.config.mjs`**: Enables `asyncWebAssembly` webpack experiment and sets `webassemblyModuleFilename`. No other customization.

**`tsconfig.json`**: Strict mode, `bundler` module resolution, `@/*` path alias to `./src/*`, no emit.

**`.env` / `.env.local`**: Both contain one variable:
```
NEXT_PUBLIC_STARKNET_RPC_URL=https://starknet-mainnet.public.blastapi.io
```

**`globals.css`**: Tailwind v4 `@theme` block with custom `falcon-*` color tokens. Light/dark class-based theming (`.dark` and `.light` on `<html>`). Smooth scroll with `prefers-reduced-motion` override. Note: `falcon-secondary` (#8b5cf6) is defined only in `ThemeToggle.tsx` BRAND_TOKENS, NOT in globals.css.

---

### 4. Services Layer (Effect-based)

#### `WasmRuntime` (`src/services/WasmRuntime.ts`)
- `Context.Tag("WasmRuntime")` — dependency injection tag for the WASM module
- `WasmModule` interface: 7 methods — `keygen`, `sign`, `verify`, `create_verification_hint`, `pack_public_key_wasm`, `public_key_length`, `salt_length`
- `isValidWasmModule(mod)` — runtime guard checking all 7 methods exist and are functions (no unsafe casts)
- `WasmRuntimeLive` — `Layer.effect` that dynamically imports `/wasm/falcon_rs.js` via `/* webpackIgnore: true */` comment, runs the default initializer, validates the module

#### `FalconService` (`src/services/FalconService.ts`)
- `Effect.Service` with 6 methods:
  - `generateKeypair(seed?)` → `FalconKeypair` (includes parsed `publicKeyNtt: Int32Array`)
  - `sign(sk, msg)` → `FalconSignatureResult`
  - `verify(vk, msg, sig)` → `boolean`
  - `createHint(s1, pkNtt)` → `Uint16Array`
  - `packPublicKey(pkNtt)` → `PackedPublicKey` (29 felt252 hex strings)
  - `deserializePublicKeyNtt(vkBytes)` → `Int32Array`
- Contains `parsePublicKeyNttBytes()` — pure JS function that deserializes Falcon VK 14-bit LSB-first packed format into 512 Int32 coefficients
- Errors: `KeygenError`, `SigningError`, `VerificationError`, `HintGenerationError`, `PackingError`
- Depends on `WasmRuntime` (injected via Layer)

#### `StarknetService` (`src/services/StarknetService.ts`)
- `Effect.Service` with 5 methods:
  - `computeDeployAddress(packedPk)` → `ContractAddress` (pure computation via starknet.js hash)
  - `getBalance(address)` → `bigint` (calls STRK token `balanceOf`)
  - `deployAccount(packedPk, privateKey)` → `{ txHash, address }`
  - `waitForTx(txHash)` → void
  - `provider` — exposes the `RpcProvider` instance
- Reads `NEXT_PUBLIC_STARKNET_RPC_URL` from Effect `Config`
- STRK token address on mainnet: `REDACTED`
- `FALCON_ACCOUNT_CLASS_HASH = "0x0"` — **PLACEHOLDER, not yet declared on chain**
- Errors: `StarknetRpcError`, `AccountDeployError`

#### `errors.ts` — 8 `Schema.TaggedError` classes:
`WasmLoadError`, `KeygenError`, `SigningError`, `VerificationError`, `HintGenerationError`, `PackingError`, `StarknetRpcError`, `AccountDeployError`, `InsufficientFundsError`

#### `types.ts` — Domain types:
- `TxHash`, `ContractAddress` — branded `Schema.String` types
- `FalconKeypair`, `FalconSignatureResult`, `PackedPublicKey`
- `VerificationStep` — tagged union with 8 variants (`idle | generating-keypair | signing | creating-hint | packing | verifying | complete | error`)
- `PipelineStep` — static pipeline step descriptor

---

### 5. Atom State (Effect-Atom)

**`atoms/falcon.ts`**:
- `wasmStatusAtom`: `"loading" | "ready" | "error"`, default `"loading"`, `keepAlive: true`
- `keypairAtom`: `Option<FalconKeypair>`, default `none`, `keepAlive: true`
- `signatureAtom`: `Option<FalconSignatureResult>`, default `none`
- `verificationStepAtom`: `VerificationStep`, default `{ step: "idle" }`
- `messageAtom`: `string`, default `""`

**`atoms/pipeline.ts`**:
- `INITIAL_PIPELINE_STEPS` — static array of 6 `PipelineStep` objects
- 6 steps: `hash-to-point` (5988), `ntt-s1` (15000), `pointwise-mul` (1500), `ntt-hint` (15000), `recover-s0` (500), `norm-check` (26000) — total: 63,988 steps
- `pipelineStepsAtom`, `pipelineActiveStepAtom` (default: -1), `pipelinePlayingAtom` (default: false) — all `keepAlive: false`

**`atoms/starknet.ts`**:
- `DeployStep` — tagged union with 8 variants (`idle | generating-keypair | packing | computing-address | awaiting-funds | deploying | deployed | error`)
- `deployStepAtom`, `deployedAddressAtom`, `deployTxHashAtom` — all `keepAlive: true`

---

### 6. Components

**Page Structure (page.tsx)**:
```
<main>
  <Hero />                  — RSC
  <WhyPostQuantum />        — RSC
  <PerformanceStats />      — RSC
  <PlaygroundSection />     — Client (dynamic, ssr:false)
  <PipelineSection />       — Client (dynamic, ssr:false)
  <AccountDeploySection />  — Client (dynamic, ssr:false)
  <Footer />                — RSC
</main>
```

**Landing components (RSC, no client JS)**:
- `Hero`: stat cards (63K steps, 62 calldata felts, 29 storage slots), "Try Verification" and "Deploy Account" CTA anchor links
- `WhyPostQuantum`: 4 cards explaining Quantum Threat, Account Abstraction, Falcon-512, Hint-Based Verification
- `PerformanceStats`: table of verify/verify_with_msg_point/hash_to_point/NTT-512 step counts and L2 gas estimates; calldata efficiency callout (17x reduction)
- `Footer`: GitHub and Starknet docs links

**Interactive components (client, loaded via `next/dynamic`)**:
- `VerificationPlayground`: Uses a single `ManagedRuntime` singleton (process-lifetime). Handlers: `handleGenerateKeypair` (calls `FalconService.generateKeypair`) and `handleSignAndVerify` (calls `sign` then `verify` — **skips createHint and packPublicKey**). Shows HexDisplay for verifying key preview. Shows success/error result cards with ARIA roles.
- `PipelineVisualizer`: Play/Pause/Step/Reset controls for 6-step animated walkthrough. 2-second interval auto-advance. No WASM connection — purely visual animation of pre-defined step data.
- `AccountDeployFlow`: 5-step wizard (`handlePrepare` calls `prepareAccountDeployEffect`, `handleDeploy` calls `deployAccountEffect`). Shows STRK address to fund. On success: address + tx hash + Starkscan link.
- `HexDisplay`: Renders single hex strings or arrays of felt252 strings. Supports `maxRows` clamping and `truncate: { head, tail }` shortening.
- `ThemeToggle`: Floating sun/moon button. EXISTS but is NOT imported in `page.tsx` or `layout.tsx` — completely unreachable.

**Pipeline logic** (`accountDeployPipeline.ts`):
- `validateHexPrivateKey` — validates 0x-prefix, 64-char hex body
- `toUint16PublicKeyNtt` — converts Int32Array to Uint16Array with range check
- `prepareAccountDeployEffect` — validates key, gets/generates keypair, packs PK, computes address
- `deployAccountEffect` — validates key, checks STRK balance, deploys account

---

### 7. Blockchain/Starknet Integration

**Library**: `starknet` v9.2.1 — `RpcProvider`, `Account`, `hash.calculateContractAddressFromHash`, `CallData.compile`, `stark.randomAddress`

**Network**: Starknet mainnet via public BlastAPI RPC (`https://starknet-mainnet.public.blastapi.io`)

**Contracts referenced**:
- STRK token: `REDACTED` (real address)
- FalconAccount class hash: `0x0` — **placeholder, Cairo contract not yet declared**

**On-chain operations attempted**:
- `balanceOf` call to STRK token contract (read, no signing)
- `account.deployAccount(...)` — write, broadcasts deploy transaction
- `provider.waitForTransaction(...)` — polls for confirmation

**No wallet connection / no browser wallet** — deploy uses a raw private key input by the user.

---

### 8. WASM Bindings

Location: `/home/felt/PycharmProjects/s2morrow/apps/demo/public/wasm/`
- `falcon_rs.js` (16.6K) — wasm-pack generated JS glue, loaded dynamically at runtime
- `falcon_rs_bg.wasm` (254.7K) — Falcon-512 Rust implementation compiled to WASM

7 exported WASM functions exposed through `WasmModule` interface:
1. `keygen(seed: Uint8Array)` → `{ sk: Uint8Array, vk: Uint8Array }` — 1281 byte SK, 896 byte VK
2. `sign(sk, msg, salt)` → `{ signature: Uint8Array, salt: Uint8Array }` — 666 byte sig, 40 byte salt
3. `verify(vk, msg, signature)` → `boolean`
4. `create_verification_hint(s1: Int32Array, pkNtt: Int32Array)` → `Uint16Array` — off-chain hint for 2-NTT verification
5. `pack_public_key_wasm(pkNtt: Uint16Array)` → `string[]` — 29 hex felt252 strings (base-Q packing)
6. `public_key_length()` → `number` (896)
7. `salt_length()` → `number` (40)

The companion Rust crate for this is at `../../falcon-rs/` (relative to the app).

---

### 9. Test Coverage

**Test runner**: `bun test`. Total: **471 tests, 0 failures, 8 todo** (per findings.md).

Test files and what they cover:

| File | Tests | What's covered |
|---|---|---|
| `services/__tests__/WasmRuntime.test.ts` | ~20 | `isValidWasmModule` guard, Layer mock injection, live Layer failure path |
| `__tests__/verify-playground/falcon-service.test.ts` | ~30 | All 5 FalconService methods, success + failure + error propagation, full pipeline integration |
| `__tests__/verify-playground/atoms.test.ts` | ~20 | All 5 falcon atoms, initial values, transitions |
| `__tests__/verify-playground/verification-utils.test.ts` | ~20 | `bytesToHex`, `truncateHex`, `getVerificationDisabledState` |
| `__tests__/verify-playground/verification-playground.test.tsx` | ~5 | SSR render, ARIA labels, button disabled states |
| `__tests__/account-deploy/account-deploy-pipeline.test.ts` | ~10 | `validateHexPrivateKey`, `toUint16PublicKeyNtt`, `prepareAccountDeployEffect`, `deployAccountEffect` |
| `__tests__/account-deploy/account-deploy-view.test.tsx` | ~3 | SSR render of AccountDeployFlow states |
| `__tests__/effect-services/starknet-service.test.ts` | ~10 | All StarknetService methods mocked + real computeDeployAddress |
| `__tests__/effect-services/starknet-errors.test.ts` | ~15 | StarknetRpcError, AccountDeployError, InsufficientFundsError constructors + catchTag |
| `__tests__/pipeline-viz.test.ts` | ~20 | INITIAL_PIPELINE_STEPS data, pipeline atom transitions |
| `tests/atoms/falcon.test.ts` | ~15 | keepAlive properties, atom defaults |
| `tests/atoms/pipeline.test.ts` | ~20 | Step counts, keepAlive, transitions, exact spec values |
| `tests/atoms/starknet.test.ts` | ~20 | Deploy step state machine, branded types, subscribe |

All tests use mock injection (no live WASM, no live RPC) except:
- `StarknetService.computeDeployAddress` real implementation test (pure hash, no network)
- `WasmRuntimeLive` failure test (expected to fail — WASM file not available in Bun)

---

### 10. Current State: What Works vs. What's Stubbed/Incomplete

**Fully implemented and working**:
- All 7 WASM sections are present and loadable
- Full service layer (FalconService, StarknetService, WasmRuntime) with proper Effect patterns
- All atom state is correctly defined and tested
- Hero, WhyPostQuantum, PerformanceStats, Footer — static RSC sections work
- PipelineVisualizer — animated walkthrough with play/pause/step/reset fully works
- HexDisplay, verification-utils — utilities fully work
- VerificationPlayground keygen → sign → verify flow — works
- Deploy wizard UI (5 steps, step indicators, error/success states) — renders correctly
- Production build passes, 471 tests passing, typecheck clean

**Bugs / Incomplete**:

1. **[BUG-01] VerificationPlayground skips hint creation and packing.** `handleSignAndVerify` in `VerificationPlayground.tsx` goes sign → verify, never calling `FalconService.createHint()` or `FalconService.packPublicKey()`. The full PRD pipeline (sign → creating-hint → packing → verify) is not implemented. The 29 packed felt252 slots and signature hex preview are never displayed. The `creating-hint` and `packing` step states exist in the type union and are tested in atoms but never triggered.

2. **[BUG-02] `FALCON_ACCOUNT_CLASS_HASH = "0x0"` in `StarknetService.ts:11`.** Any actual deploy attempt will fail immediately at the Starknet RPC level. The Cairo contract must be declared on mainnet first. No user-facing notice about this.

3. **[BUG-03] No balance polling loop.** Step 4 "Fund Account" shows the address but the user must manually click "Deploy Account" — there is no automatic polling of `StarknetService.getBalance`.

4. **[BUG-04] `computing-address` state is instantly overwritten** — lines 80–81 of `AccountDeployFlow.tsx` set `computing-address` then immediately `awaiting-funds` synchronously, so the state is never rendered.

5. **[BUG-05] `ThemeToggle` exists at `src/components/ThemeToggle.tsx` but is never imported or rendered anywhere** — `layout.tsx` hardcodes `<html className="dark">`. The toggle button is completely invisible to users.

**UX/Polish gaps** (from findings.md):
- Private key input is `type="text"` (plaintext visible) — should be `type="password"` with reveal toggle
- No copy-to-clipboard on HexDisplay
- No signature hex preview after signing
- No packed calldata (29 felt252 slots) display after the full pipeline
- Missing step 6 in the deploy wizard ("Sign test transaction with new account")
- No navigation header/nav bar
- `falcon-secondary` color token missing from `globals.css @theme` block
- No skip-to-content accessibility link

**`atoms/pipeline.ts`**:
- `INITIAL_PIPELINE_STEPS` — static array of 6 `PipelineStep` objects
- 6 steps: `hash-to-point` (5988), `ntt-s1` (15000), `pointwise-mul` (1500), `ntt-hint` (15000), `recover-s0` (500), `norm-check` (26000) — total: 63,988 steps
- `pipelineStepsAtom`, `pipelineActiveStepAtom` (default: -1), `pipelinePlayingAtom` (default: false) — all `keepAlive: false`

**`atoms/starknet.ts`**:
- `DeployStep` — tagged union with 8 variants (`idle | generating-keypair | packing | computing-address | awaiting-funds | deploying | deployed | error`)
- `deployStepAtom`, `deployedAddressAtom`, `deployTxHashAtom` — all `keepAlive: true`

---

### 6. Components

**Page Structure (page.tsx)**:
```
<main>
  <Hero />                  — RSC
  <WhyPostQuantum />        — RSC
  <PerformanceStats />      — RSC
  <PlaygroundSection />     — Client (dynamic, ssr:false)
  <PipelineSection />       — Client (dynamic, ssr:false)
  <AccountDeploySection />  — Client (dynamic, ssr:false)
  <Footer />                — RSC
</main>
```

**Landing components (RSC, no client JS)**:
- `Hero`: stat cards (63K steps, 62 calldata felts, 29 storage slots), "Try Verification" and "Deploy Account" CTA anchor links
- `WhyPostQuantum`: 4 cards explaining Quantum Threat, Account Abstraction, Falcon-512, Hint-Based Verification
- `PerformanceStats`: table of verify/verify_with_msg_point/hash_to_point/NTT-512 step counts and L2 gas estimates; calldata efficiency callout (17x reduction)
- `Footer`: GitHub and Starknet docs links

**Interactive components (client, loaded via `next/dynamic`)**:
- `VerificationPlayground`: Uses a single `ManagedRuntime` singleton (process-lifetime). Handlers: `handleGenerateKeypair` (calls `FalconService.generateKeypair`) and `handleSignAndVerify` (calls `sign` then `verify` — **skips createHint and packPublicKey**). Shows HexDisplay for verifying key preview. Shows success/error result cards with ARIA roles.
- `PipelineVisualizer`: Play/Pause/Step/Reset controls for 6-step animated walkthrough. 2-second interval auto-advance. No WASM connection — purely visual animation of pre-defined step data.
- `AccountDeployFlow`: 5-step wizard (`handlePrepare` calls `prepareAccountDeployEffect`, `handleDeploy` calls `deployAccountEffect`). Shows STRK address to fund. On success: address + tx hash + Starkscan link.
- `HexDisplay`: Renders single hex strings or arrays of felt252 strings. Supports `maxRows` clamping and `truncate: { head, tail }` shortening.
- `ThemeToggle`: Floating sun/moon button. EXISTS but is NOT imported in `page.tsx` or `layout.tsx` — completely unreachable.

**Pipeline logic** (`accountDeployPipeline.ts`):
- `validateHexPrivateKey` — validates 0x-prefix, 64-char hex body
- `toUint16PublicKeyNtt` — converts Int32Array to Uint16Array with range check
- `prepareAccountDeployEffect` — validates key, gets/generates keypair, packs PK, computes address
- `deployAccountEffect` — validates key, checks STRK balance, deploys account

---

### 7. Blockchain/Starknet Integration

**Library**: `starknet` v9.2.1 — `RpcProvider`, `Account`, `hash.calculateContractAddressFromHash`, `CallData.compile`, `stark.randomAddress`

**Network**: Starknet mainnet via public BlastAPI RPC (`https://starknet-mainnet.public.blastapi.io`)

**Contracts referenced**:
- STRK token: `REDACTED` (real address)
- FalconAccount class hash: `0x0` — **placeholder, Cairo contract not yet declared**

**On-chain operations attempted**:
- `balanceOf` call to STRK token contract (read, no signing)
- `account.deployAccount(...)` — write, broadcasts deploy transaction
- `provider.waitForTransaction(...)` — polls for confirmation

**No wallet connection / no browser wallet** — deploy uses a raw private key input by the user.

---

### 8. WASM Bindings

Location: `/home/felt/PycharmProjects/s2morrow/apps/demo/public/wasm/`
- `falcon_rs.js` (16.6K) — wasm-pack generated JS glue, loaded dynamically at runtime
- `falcon_rs_bg.wasm` (254.7K) — Falcon-512 Rust implementation compiled to WASM

7 exported WASM functions exposed through `WasmModule` interface:
1. `keygen(seed: Uint8Array)` → `{ sk: Uint8Array, vk: Uint8Array }` — 1281 byte SK, 896 byte VK
2. `sign(sk, msg, salt)` → `{ signature: Uint8Array, salt: Uint8Array }` — 666 byte sig, 40 byte salt
3. `verify(vk, msg, signature)` → `boolean`
4. `create_verification_hint(s1: Int32Array, pkNtt: Int32Array)` → `Uint16Array` — off-chain hint for 2-NTT verification
5. `pack_public_key_wasm(pkNtt: Uint16Array)` → `string[]` — 29 hex felt252 strings (base-Q packing)
6. `public_key_length()` → `number` (896)
7. `salt_length()` → `number` (40)

The companion Rust crate for this is at `../../falcon-rs/` (relative to the app).

---

### 9. Test Coverage

**Test runner**: `bun test`. Total: **471 tests, 0 failures, 8 todo** (per findings.md).

Test files and what they cover:

| File | Tests | What's covered |
|---|---|---|
| `services/__tests__/WasmRuntime.test.ts` | ~20 | `isValidWasmModule` guard, Layer mock injection, live Layer failure path |
| `__tests__/verify-playground/falcon-service.test.ts` | ~30 | All 5 FalconService methods, success + failure + error propagation, full pipeline integration |
| `__tests__/verify-playground/atoms.test.ts` | ~20 | All 5 falcon atoms, initial values, transitions |
| `__tests__/verify-playground/verification-utils.test.ts` | ~20 | `bytesToHex`, `truncateHex`, `getVerificationDisabledState` |
| `__tests__/verify-playground/verification-playground.test.tsx` | ~5 | SSR render, ARIA labels, button disabled states |
| `__tests__/account-deploy/account-deploy-pipeline.test.ts` | ~10 | `validateHexPrivateKey`, `toUint16PublicKeyNtt`, `prepareAccountDeployEffect`, `deployAccountEffect` |
| `__tests__/account-deploy/account-deploy-view.test.tsx` | ~3 | SSR render of AccountDeployFlow states |
| `__tests__/effect-services/starknet-service.test.ts` | ~10 | All StarknetService methods mocked + real computeDeployAddress |
| `__tests__/effect-services/starknet-errors.test.ts` | ~15 | StarknetRpcError, AccountDeployError, InsufficientFundsError constructors + catchTag |
| `__tests__/pipeline-viz.test.ts` | ~20 | INITIAL_PIPELINE_STEPS data, pipeline atom transitions |
| `tests/atoms/falcon.test.ts` | ~15 | keepAlive properties, atom defaults |
| `tests/atoms/pipeline.test.ts` | ~20 | Step counts, keepAlive, transitions, exact spec values |
| `tests/atoms/starknet.test.ts` | ~20 | Deploy step state machine, branded types, subscribe |

All tests use mock injection (no live WASM, no live RPC) except:
- `StarknetService.computeDeployAddress` real implementation test (pure hash, no network)
- `WasmRuntimeLive` failure test (expected to fail — WASM file not available in Bun)

---

### 10. Current State: What Works vs. What's Stubbed/Incomplete

**Fully implemented and working**:
- All 7 WASM sections are present and loadable
- Full service layer (FalconService, StarknetService, WasmRuntime) with proper Effect patterns
- All atom state is correctly defined and tested
- Hero, WhyPostQuantum, PerformanceStats, Footer — static RSC sections work
- PipelineVisualizer — animated walkthrough with play/pause/step/reset fully works
- HexDisplay, verification-utils — utilities fully work
- VerificationPlayground keygen → sign → verify flow — works
- Deploy wizard UI (5 steps, step indicators, error/success states) — renders correctly
- Production build passes, 471 tests passing, typecheck clean

**Bugs / Incomplete**:

1. **[BUG-01] VerificationPlayground skips hint creation and packing.** `handleSignAndVerify` in `VerificationPlayground.tsx` goes sign → verify, never calling `FalconService.createHint()` or `FalconService.packPublicKey()`. The full PRD pipeline (sign → creating-hint → packing → verify) is not implemented. The 29 packed felt252 slots and signature hex preview are never displayed. The `creating-hint` and `packing` step states exist in the type union and are tested in atoms but never triggered.

2. **[BUG-02] `FALCON_ACCOUNT_CLASS_HASH = "0x0"` in `StarknetService.ts:11`.** Any actual deploy attempt will fail immediately at the Starknet RPC level. The Cairo contract must be declared on mainnet first. No user-facing notice about this.

3. **[BUG-03] No balance polling loop.** Step 4 "Fund Account" shows the address but the user must manually click "Deploy Account" — there is no automatic polling of `StarknetService.getBalance`.

4. **[BUG-04] `computing-address` state is instantly overwritten** — lines 80–81 of `AccountDeployFlow.tsx` set `computing-address` then immediately `awaiting-funds` synchronously, so the state is never rendered.

5. **[BUG-05] `ThemeToggle` exists at `src/components/ThemeToggle.tsx` but is never imported or rendered anywhere** — `layout.tsx` hardcodes `<html className="dark">`. The toggle button is completely invisible to users.

**UX/Polish gaps** (from findings.md):
- Private key input is `type="text"` (plaintext visible) — should be `type="password"` with reveal toggle
- No copy-to-clipboard on HexDisplay
- No signature hex preview after signing
- No packed calldata (29 felt252 slots) display after the full pipeline
- Missing step 6 in the deploy wizard ("Sign test transaction with new account")
- No navigation header/nav bar
- `falcon-secondary` color token missing from `globals.css @theme` block
- No skip-to-content accessibility link

1→import { Config, Effect } from "effect"
     2→import { RpcProvider, Account, hash, CallData, stark } from "starknet"
     3→import {
     4→  StarknetRpcError,
     5→  AccountDeployError,
     6→} from "./errors"
     7→import { TxHash, ContractAddress } from "./types"
     8→import type { PackedPublicKey } from "./types"
     9→
    10→// FalconAccount class hash — must be declared on mainnet first
    11→const FALCON_ACCOUNT_CLASS_HASH = "0x0" // TODO: replace after declaring
    12→
    13→/** STRK token contract address on Starknet mainnet */
    14→export const STRK_TOKEN_ADDRESS =
    15→  "0x04718f5a0fc34cc1af16a1cdee98ffb20c31f5cd61d6ab07201858f4287c938d"
    16→
    17→export class StarknetService extends Effect.Service<StarknetService>()(
    18→  "StarknetService",
    19→  {
    20→    accessors: true,
    21→    effect: Effect.gen(function* () {
    22→      const rpcUrl = yield* Config.string("NEXT_PUBLIC_STARKNET_RPC_URL")
    23→      const provider = new RpcProvider({ nodeUrl: rpcUrl })
    24→
    25→      const computeDeployAddress = Effect.fn("Starknet.computeDeployAddress")(
    26→        function* (packedPk: PackedPublicKey) {
    27→          const constructorCalldata = CallData.compile({
    28→            pk_packed: packedPk.slots,
    29→          })
    30→          const salt = stark.randomAddress()
    31→          const address = hash.calculateContractAddressFromHash(
    32→            salt,
    33→            FALCON_ACCOUNT_CLASS_HASH,
    34→            constructorCalldata,
    35→            0,
    36→          )
    37→          return ContractAddress.make(address)
    38→        },
    39→      )
    40→
    41→      const getBalance = Effect.fn("Starknet.getBalance")(
    42→        function* (address: string) {
    43→          return yield* Effect.tryPromise({
    44→            try: async () => {
    45→              const result = await provider.callContract({
    46→                contractAddress: STRK_TOKEN_ADDRESS,
    47→                entrypoint: "balanceOf",
    48→                calldata: [address],
    49→              })
    50→              return BigInt(result[0])
    51→            },
    52→            catch: (error) =>
    53→              new StarknetRpcError({ message: String(error), code: -1 }),
    54→          })
    55→        },
    56→      )
    57→
    58→      const deployAccount = Effect.fn("Starknet.deployAccount")(
    59→        function* (packedPk: PackedPublicKey, privateKey: string) {
    60→          const constructorCalldata = CallData.compile({
    61→            pk_packed: packedPk.slots,
    62→          })
    63→          const salt = stark.randomAddress()
    64→          const address = hash.calculateContractAddressFromHash(
    65→            salt,
    66→            FALCON_ACCOUNT_CLASS_HASH,
    67→            constructorCalldata,
    68→            0,
    69→          )
    70→
    71→          // starknet v9 Account constructor takes a single AccountOptions object
    72→          const account = new Account({ provider, address, signer: privateKey })
    73→
    74→          const result = yield* Effect.tryPromise({
    75→            try: () =>
    76→              account.deployAccount({
    77→                classHash: FALCON_ACCOUNT_CLASS_HASH,
    78→                constructorCalldata,
    79→                addressSalt: salt,
    80→                contractAddress: address,
    81→              }),
    82→            catch: (error) =>
    83→              new AccountDeployError({ message: String(error) }),
    84→          })
    85→
    86→          yield* Effect.tryPromise({
    87→            try: () => provider.waitForTransaction(result.transaction_hash),
    88→            catch: (error) =>
    89→              new AccountDeployError({
    90→                message: `Tx failed: ${error}`,
    91→                txHash: result.transaction_hash,
    92→              }),
    93→          })
    94→
    95→          return {
    96→            txHash: TxHash.make(result.transaction_hash),
    97→            address: ContractAddress.make(result.contract_address),
    98→          }
    99→        },
   100→      )
   101→
   102→      const waitForTx = Effect.fn("Starknet.waitForTx")(
   103→        function* (txHash: string) {
   104→          yield* Effect.tryPromise({
   105→            try: () => provider.waitForTransaction(txHash),
   106→            catch: (error) =>
   107→              new StarknetRpcError({ message: String(error), code: -1 }),
   108→          })
   109→        },
   110→      )
   111→
   112→      return {
   113→        computeDeployAddress,
   114→        getBalance,
   115→        deployAccount,
   116→        waitForTx,
   117→        provider,
   118→      }
   119→    }),
   120→  },
   121→) {}
   122→

Type a message to sign 9→ * 3. Run sign + verify and see the result with timing 10→ * 11→ * Architecture: 12→ * - Atom state (keypairAtom, verificationStepAtom, messageAtom, etc.) drives 13→ * all UI updates — no local useState. 14→ * - FalconService methods are run via a ManagedRuntime (singleton defined 15→ * outside the component) so the WASM layer is loaded at most once. 16→ * - All Option values are handled via
