content.content:
1028
---
name: cairo-deploy
description: Use when deploying Cairo contracts to Starknet — sncast commands, account setup, declare/deploy workflow, network configuration, contract verification.
license: Apache-2.0
metadata: {"author":"starknet-agentic","version":"1.0.0","org":"keep-starknet-strange"}
keywords: [cairo, deploy, sncast, starknet, devnet, sepolia, mainnet, declare, verification]
allowed-tools: [Bash, Read, Write, Glob, Grep, Task]
user-invocable: true
---

# Cairo Deploy

Reference for deploying Cairo smart contracts to Starknet using sncast (Starknet Foundry).

## When to Use

- Deploying contracts to Starknet devnet, Sepolia, or mainnet
- Declaring contract classes
- Setting up deployer accounts
- Configuring network endpoints
- Verifying deployed contracts
- Invoking/calling deployed contracts

**Not for:** Writing contracts (use cairo-contracts), testing (use cairo-testing), optimization (use cairo-optimization)

## Setup

### Install Starknet Foundry

```bash
# Install via asdf (recommended for version pinning)
asdf plugin add starknet-foundry
asdf install starknet-foundry 0.56.0
asdf global starknet-foundry 0.56.0

# Or install directly
curl -L https://raw.githubusercontent.com/foundry-rs/starknet-foundry/master/scripts/install.sh | sh
snfoundryup
```

### .tool-versions

Pin versions for reproducible builds:

```
scarb 2.15.1
starknet-foundry 0.56.0
```

> **Note:** snforge 0.56.0 requires Scarb >= 2.12.0. Check [github.com/foundry-rs/starknet-foundry/releases](https://github.com/foundry-rs/starknet-foundry/releases) for the latest.

## Build

```bash
# Build contracts (generates Sierra + CASM)
scarb build
```

Output goes to `target/dev/`:
- `myproject_MyContract.contract_class.json` (Sierra)
- `myproject_MyContract.compiled_contract_class.json` (CASM)

## Account Setup

### Create a New Account

```bash
# Generate account on Sepolia
sncast account create \
    --url https://starknet-sepolia.g.alchemy.com/v2/YOUR_KEY \
    --name my-deployer

# This outputs the account address — fund it with ETH/STRK before deploying

# Deploy the account contract
sncast account deploy \
    --url https://starknet-sepolia.g.alchemy.com/v2/YOUR_KEY \
    --name my-deployer
```

### Import Existing Account

```bash
sncast account add \
    --url https://starknet-sepolia.g.alchemy.com/v2/YOUR_KEY \
    --name my-deployer \
    --address 0x123... \
    --private-key 0xabc... \
    --type oz
```

Account types: `oz` (OpenZeppelin), `argent`, `braavos`

## sncast.toml

Configure defaults to avoid repeating flags:

```toml
[default]
url = "https://starknet-sepolia.g.alchemy.com/v2/YOUR_KEY"
account = "my-deployer"
accounts-file = "~/.starknet_accounts/starknet_open_zeppelin_accounts.json"
wait = true

[mainnet]
url = "https://starknet-mainnet.g.alchemy.com/v2/YOUR_KEY"
account = "mainnet-deployer"
```

Use profiles: `sncast --profile mainnet declare ...`

## Declare (Register Class)

Before deploying, declare the contract class on-chain:

```bash
# Declare contract
sncast declare \
    --contract-name MyContract

# Output:
# class_hash: 0x1234...
# transaction_hash: 0xabcd...
```

If the class is already declared, sncast will tell you — that's fine, use the existing class hash.

## Deploy (Create Instance)

```bash
# Deploy with constructor args
sncast deploy \
    --class-hash 0x1234... \
    --constructor-calldata 0xOWNER_ADDRESS

# Multiple constructor args (space-separated)
sncast deploy \
    --class-hash 0x1234... \
    --constructor-calldata 0xOWNER 0xTOKEN_ADDRESS 1000
```

### Constructor Calldata Encoding

Arguments are passed as felt252 values:
- `ContractAddress` — pass as hex `0x123...`
- `u256` — pass as TWO felts: `low high` (e.g., `1000 0` for 1000)
- `felt252` — pass directly
- `bool` — `1` for true, `0` for false
- `ByteArray` (strings) — use sncast's string encoding or pass raw

## Invoke (Write)

```bash
# Call a write function
sncast invoke \
    --contract-address 0xCONTRACT \
    --function "transfer" \
    --calldata 0xRECIPIENT 1000 0
```

## Call (Read)

```bash
# Call a view function (free, no tx)
sncast call \
    --contract-address 0xCONTRACT \
    --function "get_balance" \
    --calldata 0xACCOUNT
```

## Multicall

Execute multiple calls in a single transaction:

```bash
# Create a multicall file
cat > multicall.toml << 'EOF'
[[call]]
call_type = "deploy"
class_hash = "0x1234..."
inputs = ["0xOWNER"]

[[call]]
call_type = "invoke"
contract_address = "0xTOKEN"
function = "approve"
inputs = ["0xSPENDER", "1000", "0"]
EOF

sncast multicall run --path multicall.toml
```

## Deploy Script Pattern

For complex deployments, use a script:

```bash
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

RPC_URL="https://starknet-sepolia.g.alchemy.com/v2/YOUR_KEY"
ACCOUNT="my-deployer"

echo "Building..."
scarb build

echo "Declaring MyToken..."
TOKEN_CLASS=$(sncast declare --contract-name MyToken --url $RPC_URL --account $ACCOUNT | grep "class_hash" | awk '{print $2}')
echo "Token class: $TOKEN_CLASS"

echo "Deploying MyToken..."
TOKEN_ADDR=$(sncast deploy --class-hash $TOKEN_CLASS --constructor-calldata 0xOWNER --url $RPC_URL --account $ACCOUNT | grep "contract_address" | awk '{print $2}')
echo "Token deployed at: $TOKEN_ADDR"

echo "Declaring AMM..."
AMM_CLASS=$(sncast declare --contract-name AMM --url $RPC_URL --account $ACCOUNT | grep "class_hash" | awk '{print $2}')

echo "Deploying AMM..."
AMM_ADDR=$(sncast deploy --class-hash $AMM_CLASS --constructor-calldata $TOKEN_ADDR --url $RPC_URL --account $ACCOUNT | grep "contract_address" | awk '{print $2}')
echo "AMM deployed at: $AMM_ADDR"

echo "Done. Addresses:"
echo "  Token: $TOKEN_ADDR"
echo "  AMM:   $AMM_ADDR"
```

## Network Endpoints

| Network | RPC URL |
|---------|---------|
| Devnet (local) | `http://localhost:5050` |
| Sepolia (testnet) | `https://starknet-sepolia.g.alchemy.com/v2/KEY` |
| Mainnet | `https://starknet-mainnet.g.alchemy.com/v2/KEY` |

Alternative providers: Infura, Blast, Nethermind (free tier available).

### Local Devnet

```bash
# Install and run starknet-devnet-rs
cargo install starknet-devnet
starknet-devnet --seed 42

# Devnet provides pre-funded accounts — use them for testing
```

## Contract Verification

Verify source code on Voyager or Starkscan:

```bash
# Verify on Voyager (manual: upload Sierra JSON via web UI)
# https://sepolia.voyager.online/contract/0xADDRESS#code

# Or use Walnut for programmatic verification
# https://app.walnut.dev
```

> **Note:** `sncast verify` currently only supports the Walnut verification backend. Voyager and Starkscan verification must be done through their respective web UIs.

## Upgradeable Contracts

For contracts using OZ UpgradeableComponent:

```bash
# 1. Declare new class
sncast declare --contract-name MyContractV2

# 2. Call upgrade on existing contract
sncast invoke \
    --contract-address 0xEXISTING_CONTRACT \
    --function "upgrade" \
    --calldata 0xNEW_CLASS_HASH
```

## Common Errors

| Error | Cause | Fix |
|-------|-------|-----|
| `Contract not found` | Account not deployed | Run `sncast account deploy` |
| `Insufficient max fee` | Not enough ETH/STRK for gas | Fund the deployer account |
| `Class already declared` | Same class hash exists | Use the existing class hash for deploy |
| `Entry point not found` | Wrong function name | Check the contract ABI |
| `Invalid calldata` | Wrong number/type of args | Check constructor signature, remember u256 = 2 felts |

content.content:
1→---
     2→name: starknet-js
     3→description: "Guide for building Starknet applications using starknet.js v9.x SDK. Use when developing Starknet dApps, interacting with smart contracts, managing accounts, handling transactions, estimating fees, integrating browser wallets, or working with Paymaster for sponsored/alternative gas token transactions."
     4→license: Apache-2.0
     5→metadata:
     6→  author: 0xlny
     7→  version: "1.0.0"
     8→  org: keep-starknet-strange
     9→compatibility: "Node.js 18+, TypeScript 5+, npm package: starknet@^9.0.0"
    10→keywords:
    11→  - starknet
    12→  - starknet-js
    13→  - sdk
    14→  - typescript
    15→  - smart-contracts
    16→  - account-abstraction
    17→  - paymaster
    18→  - multicall
    19→  - snip-9
    20→  - snip-12
    21→  - erc-20
    22→  - erc-721
    23→  - wallet
    24→  - rpc
    25→  - fee-estimation
    26→allowed-tools:
    27→  - Bash
    28→  - Read
    29→  - Write
    30→  - Glob
    31→
    32→... (459 more lines, 13669 bytes total)
    33→

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

content.content:
1→---
     2→name: starknet-js
     3→description: "Guide for building Starknet applications using starknet.js v9.x SDK. Use when developing Starknet dApps, interacting with smart contracts, managing accounts, handling transactions, estimating fees, integrating browser wallets, or working with Paymaster for sponsored/alternative gas token transactions."
     4→license: Apache-2.0
     5→metadata:
     6→  author: 0xlny
     7→  version: "1.0.0"
     8→  org: keep-starknet-strange
     9→compatibility: "Node.js 18+, TypeScript 5+, npm package: starknet@^9.0.0"
    10→keywords:
    11→  - starknet
    12→  - starknet-js
    13→  - sdk
    14→  - typescript
    15→  - smart-contracts
    16→  - account-abstraction
    17→  - paymaster
    18→  - multicall
    19→  - snip-9
    20→  - snip-12
    21→  - erc-20
    22→  - erc-721
    23→  - wallet
    24→  - rpc
    25→  - fee-estimation
    26→allowed-tools:
    27→  - Bash
    28→  - Read
    29→  - Write
    30→  - Glob
    31→  - Grep
    32→  - Task
    33→user-invocable: true
    34→---
    35→
    36→# starknet.js v9.x SDK
    37→
    38→## Quick Start
    39→
    40→```bash
    41→npm install starknet
    42→```
    43→
    44→Minimal setup to read from Starknet:
    45→
    46→```typescript
    47→import { RpcProvider, Contract } from 'starknet';
    48→
    49→const provider = await RpcProvider.create({ nodeUrl: 'https://rpc.starknet.lava.build' });
    50→const contract = new Contract(abi, contractAddress, provider);
    51→const result = await contract.get_balance();
    52→```
    53→
    54→## Core Architecture
    55→
    56→```
    57→Provider -> Account -> Contract
    58→   |          |          |
    59→Network   Identity   Interaction
    60→```
    61→
    62→- **Provider**: Read-only network connection (RpcProvider)
    63→- **Account**: Extends Provider with signing and transaction capabilities
    64→- **Contract**: Type-safe interface to deployed contracts
    65→
    66→Use Provider for read operations, Account for write operations.
    67→
    68→## Provider Setup
    69→
    70→```typescript
    71→import { RpcProvider } from 'starknet';
    72→
    73→// Recommended: Auto-detect RPC spec version
    74→const provider = await RpcProvider.create({
    75→  nodeUrl: 'https://rpc.starknet.lava.build'
    76→});
    77→```
    78→
    79→**Networks:**
    80→- Mainnet: `https://rpc.starknet.lava.build`
    81→- Sepolia: `https://rpc.starknet-testnet.lava.build`
    82→
    83→**Key Methods:**
    84→```typescript
    85→const chainId = await provider.getChainId();
    86→const block = await provider.getBlock('latest');
    87→const nonce = await provider.getNonceForAddress(accountAddress);
    88→await provider.waitForTransaction(txHash);
    89→
    90→// Read storage directly
    91→const value = await provider.getStorageAt(contractAddress, storageKey);
    92→```
    93→
    94→## Account Management
    95→
    96→### Account Creation (4 Steps)
    97→
    98→**Step 1: Compute address**
    99→```typescript
   100→import { hash, ec, encode, CallData } from 'starknet';
   101→
   102→// IMPORTANT: `stark.randomAddress()` returns an address-like random felt and is NOT a private key.
   103→// Use a real stark curve private key generator.
   104→const privateKey = '0x' + encode.buf2hex(ec.starkCurve.utils.randomPrivateKey());
   105→const publicKey = ec.starkCurve.getStarkKey(privateKey);
   106→
   107→// NOTE: account class hashes are network/account-type dependent.
   108→// Treat this as an example only (verify the correct class hash for your setup).
   109→const classHash = '0x540d7f5ec7ecf317e68d48564934cb99259781b1ee3cedbbc37ec5337f8e688'; // example
   110→
   111→const constructorCalldata = CallData.compile({ publicKey });
   112→const address = hash.calculateContractAddressFromHash(publicKey, classHash, constructorCalldata, 0);
   113→```
   114→
   115→**Step 2: Fund the address** with STRK before deployment.
   116→
   117→**Step 3: Deploy**
   118→```typescript
   119→import { Account } from 'starknet';
   120→
   121→// NOTE: Account constructor signature varies across starknet.js versions.
   122→// If this doesn't typecheck for your version, refer to the official docs.
   123→const account = new Account({ provider, address, signer: privateKey, cairoVersion: '1' });
   124→const { transaction_hash } = await account.deployAccount({
   125→  classHash,
   126→  constructorCalldata,
   127→  addressSalt: publicKey
   128→});
   129→await provider.waitForTransaction(transaction_hash);
   130→```
   131→
   132→**Step 4: Use the account** for transactions.
   133→
   134→### Connect to Existing Account
   135→
   136→```typescript
   137→const account = new Account({
   138→  provider,
   139→  address: '0x123...',
   140→  signer: privateKey,
   141→  cairoVersion: '1'  // Optional, auto-detected if omitted
   142→});
   143→```
   144→
   145→## Contract Interaction
   146→
   147→### Connect to Contract
   148→
   149→```typescript
   150→import { Contract } from 'starknet';
   151→
   152→const contract = new Contract(abi, contractAddress, provider);  // Read-only
   153→const writeContract = new Contract(abi, contractAddress, account);   // Read-write
   154→```
   155→
   156→### Typed Contract (Type-Safe)
   157→
   158→```typescript
   159→// Get full TypeScript autocomplete and type checking from ABI
   160→const typedContract = contract.typedv2(abi);
   161→const balance = await typedContract.balanceOf(userAddress);
   162→```
   163→
   164→### Read State
   165→
   166→```typescript
   167→const balance = await contract.get_balance();
   168→const userBalance = await contract.balanceOf(userAddress);
   169→```
   170→
   171→### Write (Execute)
   172→
   173→```typescript
   174→const tx = await contract.increase_balance(100);
   175→await provider.waitForTransaction(tx.transaction_hash);
   176→```
   177→
   178→### Multicall (Batch Transactions)
   179→
   180→```typescript
   181→import { CallData, cairo } from 'starknet';
   182→
   183→const calls = [
   184→  {
   185→    contractAddress: tokenAddress,
   186→    entrypoint: 'approve',
   187→    calldata: CallData.compile({ spender: bridgeAddress, amount: cairo.uint256(1000n) })
   188→  },
   189→  {
   190→    contractAddress: bridgeAddress,
   191→    entrypoint: 'deposit',
   192→    calldata: CallData.compile({ amount: cairo.uint256(1000n) })
   193→  }
   194→];
   195→
   196→const tx = await account.execute(calls);
   197→```
   198→
   199→Using `populate()` for type-safety:
   200→```typescript
   201→const approveCall = tokenContract.populate('approve', {
   202→  spender: bridgeAddress,
   203→  amount: cairo.uint256(1000n)
   204→});
   205→const depositCall = bridgeContract.populate('deposit', { amount: cairo.uint256(1000n) });
   206→const tx = await account.execute([approveCall, depositCall]);
   207→```
   208→
   209→### Parse Events
   210→
   211→```typescript
   212→const receipt = await provider.getTransactionReceipt(txHash);
   213→const events = contract.parseEvents(receipt);
   214→const transferEvents = contract.parseEvents(receipt, 'Transfer');
   215→```
   216→
   217→## Transaction Simulation
   218→
   219→Simulate before executing to catch reverts and inspect state changes:
   220→
   221→```typescript
   222→const simResult = await account.simulateTransaction(
   223→  [{ type: 'INVOKE', payload: calls }],
   224→  { skipValidate: false }
   225→);
   226→
   227→console.log('Fee estimate:', simResult[0].fee_estimation);
   228→console.log('Trace:', simResult[0].transaction_trace);
   229→
   230→// Check state changes before execution
   231→const trace = simResult[0].transaction_trace;
   232→if (trace?.state_diff) {
   233→  console.log('Storage changes:', trace.state_diff.storage_diffs);
   234→}
   235→```
   236→
   237→## Fee Estimation
   238→
   239→```typescript
   240→const fee = await account.estimateInvokeFee(calls);
   241→console.log({
   242→  overallFee: fee.overall_fee,
   243→  resourceBounds: fee.resourceBounds  // V3: l1_gas, l2_gas, l1_data_gas
   244→});
   245→```
   246→
   247→Execute with custom bounds:
   248→```typescript
   249→const tx = await account.execute(calls, {
   250→  resourceBounds: {
   251→    l1_gas: { amount: '0x2000', price: '0x1000000000' },
   252→    l2_gas: { amount: '0x0', price: '0x0' },
   253→    l1_data_gas: { amount: '0x1000', price: '0x1000000000' }
   254→  }
   255→});
   256→```
   257→
   258→With priority tip:
   259→```typescript
   260→const tipStats = await provider.getEstimateTip();
   261→const tx = await account.execute(calls, { tip: tipStats.percentile_75 });
   262→```
   263→
   264→## Transaction Receipt Handling
   265→
   266→```typescript
   267→const receipt = await provider.waitForTransaction(txHash);
   268→
   269→// Status check helpers
   270→if (receipt.isSuccess()) {
   271→  console.log('Transaction succeeded');
   272→} else if (receipt.isReverted()) {
   273→  console.log('Reverted:', receipt.revert_reason);
   274→} else if (receipt.isRejected()) {
   275→  console.log('Rejected');
   276→} else if (receipt.isError()) {
   277→  console.log('Error');
   278→}
   279→```
   280→
   281→## Wallet Integration
   282→
   283→Connect to browser wallets (ArgentX, Braavos):
   284→
   285→```typescript
   286→import { connect } from '@starknet-io/get-starknet';
   287→import { WalletAccount } from 'starknet';
   288→
   289→const selectedWallet = await connect({ modalMode: 'alwaysAsk' });
   290→const walletAccount = await WalletAccount.connect(
   291→  { nodeUrl: 'https://rpc.starknet.lava.build' },
   292→  selectedWallet
   293→);
   294→
   295→// Use like regular Account
   296→const tx = await walletAccount.execute(calls);
   297→
   298→// Event handlers
   299→walletAccount.onAccountChange((accounts) => console.log('New account:', accounts[0]));
   300→walletAccount.onNetworkChanged((chainId) => console.log('Network changed:', chainId));
   301→```
   302→
   303→## Paymaster (Gas Sponsorship)
   304→
   305→Setup paymaster for sponsored or alternative gas token transactions:
   306→
   307→```typescript
   308→import { PaymasterRpc, Account } from 'starknet';
   309→
   310→const paymaster = new PaymasterRpc({ nodeUrl: 'https://sepolia.paymaster.avnu.fi' });
   311→const account = new Account({ provider, address, signer: privateKey, paymaster });
   312→```
   313→
   314→**Sponsored (dApp pays gas):**
   315→```typescript
   316→const tx = await account.executePaymasterTransaction(calls, { feeMode: { mode: 'sponsored' } });
   317→```
   318→
   319→**Alternative token (e.g., USDC):**
   320→```typescript
   321→const tokens = await account.paymaster.getSupportedTokens();
   322→const feeDetails = { feeMode: { mode: 'default', gasToken: USDC_ADDRESS } };
   323→const estimate = await account.estimatePaymasterTransactionFee(calls, feeDetails);
   324→const tx = await account.executePaymasterTransaction(calls, feeDetails, estimate.suggested_max_fee_in_gas_token);
   325→```
   326→
   327→## Message Signing (SNIP-12)
   328→
   329→```typescript
   330→const typedData = {
   331→  types: {
   332→    StarknetDomain: [
   333→      { name: 'name', type: 'shortstring' },
   334→      { name: 'version', type: 'shortstring' },
   335→      { name: 'chainId', type: 'shortstring' },
   336→      { name: 'revision', type: 'shortstring' }
   337→    ],
   338→    Message: [{ name: 'content', type: 'shortstring' }]
   339→  },
   340→  primaryType: 'Message',
   341→  domain: { name: 'MyDapp', version: '1', chainId: 'SN_SEPOLIA', revision: '1' },
   342→  message: { content: 'Hello Starknet' }
   343→};
   344→
   345→const signature = await account.signMessage(typedData);
   346→const msgHash = await account.hashMessage(typedData);
   347→const isValid = ec.starkCurve.verify(signature, msgHash, publicKey);
   348→```
   349→
   350→## CallData & Cairo Types
   351→
   352→```typescript
   353→import { CallData, cairo, CairoCustomEnum, CairoOption, CairoOptionVariant } from 'starknet';
   354→
   355→// Compile with ABI
   356→const calldata = new CallData(abi);
   357→const compiled = calldata.compile('transfer', { recipient: '0x...', amount: cairo.uint256(1000n) });
   358→
   359→// Cairo type helpers - always use BigInt (n suffix) for token amounts
   360→cairo.uint256(1000n)          // { low, high } - ALWAYS use BigInt for precision
   361→cairo.felt252(1000)           // BigInt
   362→cairo.felt('0x123')           // hex to felt
   363→cairo.bool(true)              // Cairo bool
   364→cairo.byteArray('Hello')      // ByteArray for long strings
   365→
   366→// Short strings (<= 31 chars)
   367→import { shortString } from 'starknet';
   368→shortString.encodeShortString('hello')  // felt252
   369→shortString.decodeShortString('0x...')  // 'hello'
   370→
   371→// Enums and Options
   372→const myEnum = new CairoCustomEnum({ Variant1: { value: 123 } });
   373→const some = new CairoOption(CairoOptionVariant.Some, value);
   374→```
   375→
   376→**Important:** Always use `BigInt` (e.g., `1000n`) for token amounts and balances. Never use `Number()` or `parseFloat()` on wei values -- JavaScript numbers lose precision above 2^53.
   377→
   378→## ERC-20 Token Operations
   379→
   380→```typescript
   381→const erc20 = new Contract(erc20Abi, tokenAddress, account);
   382→
   383→// Read balance (returns BigInt - do NOT convert with Number())
   384→const balance = await erc20.balanceOf(account.address);
   385→console.log('Balance (wei):', balance.toString());
   386→
   387→// Transfer (use BigInt for amount)
   388→const amount = cairo.uint256(1000000000000000000n); // 1 token (18 decimals)
   389→const tx = await erc20.transfer(recipientAddress, amount);
   390→await provider.waitForTransaction(tx.transaction_hash);
   391→
   392→// Approve + transferFrom pattern
   393→await erc20.approve(spenderAddress, cairo.uint256(amount));
   394→```
   395→
   396→## Utility Functions
   397→
   398→```typescript
   399→import { stark, ec, encode, num, hash } from 'starknet';
   400→
   401→// Key generation
   402→const privateKey = '0x' + encode.buf2hex(ec.starkCurve.utils.randomPrivateKey());
   403→const publicKey = ec.starkCurve.getStarkKey(privateKey);
   404→
   405→// Number conversions
   406→num.toHex(123);           // '0x7b'
   407→num.toBigInt('0x7b');     // 123n
   408→
   409→// Hashing
   410→hash.getSelectorFromName('transfer');
   411→hash.calculateContractAddressFromHash(salt, classHash, calldata, deployer);
   412→```
   413→
   414→## Contract Deployment
   415→
   416→```typescript
   417→// Deploy via UDC
   418→const { transaction_hash, contract_address } = await account.deploy({
   419→  classHash: '0x...',
   420→  constructorCalldata: CallData.compile({ owner: account.address }),
   421→  salt: stark.randomAddress(), // random felt252 salt (not a private key)
   422→  unique: true
   423→});
   424→
   425→// Declare first, then deploy
   426→const declareResponse = await account.declare({
   427→  contract: compiledSierra,
   428→  casm: compiledCasm
   429→});
   430→await provider.waitForTransaction(declareResponse.transaction_hash);
   431→
   432→const deployResponse = await account.deploy({
   433→  classHash: declareResponse.class_hash,
   434→  constructorCalldata: CallData.compile({ owner: account.address })
   435→});
   436→
   437→// Or combined
   438→const result = await account.declareAndDeploy({
   439→  contract: compiledContract,
   440→  casm: compiledCasm,
   441→  constructorCalldata: CallData.compile({ owner: account.address })
   442→});
   443→```
   444→
   445→## Outside Execution (SNIP-9)
   446→
   447→Execute transactions on behalf of another account (gasless/delegated):
   448→
   449→```typescript
   450→const version = await account.getSnip9Version();  // 'V1' | 'V2' | 'UNSUPPORTED'
   451→
   452→const outsideTransaction = await account.getOutsideTransaction(
   453→  { caller: executorAddress, execute_after: now, execute_before: now + 3600 },
   454→  calls,
   455→  'V2'
   456→);
   457→
   458→// Executor submits the pre-signed transaction
   459→const result = await executorAccount.executeFromOutside(outsideTransaction);
   460→```
   461→
   462→## Error Handling
   463→
   464→```typescript
   465→import { LibraryError, RpcError } from 'starknet';
   466→
   467→try {
   468→  const tx = await account.execute(calls);
   469→} catch (error) {
   470→  if (error instanceof RpcError) {
   471→    console.error('RPC error:', error.code, error.message);
   472→  } else if (error instanceof LibraryError) {
   473→    console.error('Library error:', error.message);
   474→  }
   475→}
   476→```
   477→
   478→## Logging & Configuration
   479→
   480→```typescript
   481→import { config, setLogLevel } from 'starknet';
   482→
   483→// Global config
   484→config.set('transactionVersion', '0x3');
   485→config.get('transactionVersion');
   486→
   487→// Logging
   488→setLogLevel('DEBUG');  // ERROR | WARN | INFO | DEBUG
   489→```
   490→

<system-reminder>

But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.

content.content:
1→---
     2→name: starknet-wallet
     3→description: Create and manage Starknet wallets for AI agents. Transfer tokens, check balances, manage session keys, deploy accounts, and interact with smart contracts using native Account Abstraction.
     4→license: Apache-2.0
     5→metadata: {"author":"starknet-agentic","version":"1.0.0","org":"keep-starknet-strange"}
     6→keywords: [starknet, wallet, transfer, balance, session-keys, account-abstraction, paymaster, gasless, agent-wallet, strk, eth]
     7→allowed-tools: [Bash, Read, Write, Glob, Grep, Task]
     8→user-invocable: true
     9→---
    10→
    11→# Starknet Wallet Skill
    12→
    13→Manage Starknet wallets for AI agents with native Account Abstraction support.
    14→
    15→## Prerequisites
    16→
    17→```bash
    18→npm install starknet@^8.9.1 @avnu/avnu-sdk@^4.0.1
    19→```
    20→
    21→Environment variables:
    22→```
    23→STARKNET_RPC_URL=https://starknet-mainnet.g.alchemy.com/v2/YOUR_KEY
    24→STARKNET_ACCOUNT_ADDRESS=0x...
    25→STARKNET_PRIVATE_KEY=0x...
    26→AVNU_BASE_URL=https://starknet.api.avnu.fi (optional)
    27→AVNU_PAYMASTER_URL=https://starknet.paymaster.avnu.fi (optional)
    28→AVNU_PAYMASTER_API_KEY=your_key (optional, for free gas)
    29→```
    30→
    31→## Available MCP Tools
    32→
    33→The Starknet MCP Server provides these tools for wallet operations:
    34→
    35→| Tool | Purpose | Key Features |
    36→|------|---------|--------------|
    37→| `starknet_get_balance` | Check single token balance | Simple, fast queries |
    38→| `starknet_get_balances` | Check multiple token balances | Batch queries (up to 200 tokens), single RPC call |
    39→| `starknet_transfer` | Send tokens | Supports gasless mode (paymaster) |
    40→| `starknet_call_contract` | Read contract state | Call view functions |
    41→| `starknet_invoke_contract` | Execute contract functions | Write operations, supports gasless |
    42→| `starknet_swap` | Execute token swaps | AVNU integration, best price routing |
    43→| `starknet_get_quote` | Get swap quotes | Price estimation before swap |
    44→| `starknet_register_agent` | Register agent identity | ERC-8004 on-chain identity |
    45→
    46→### Balance Tools Detail
    47→
    48→#### starknet_get_balance (Single Token)
    49→
    50→Query balance for one token. Use for simple cases.
    51→
    52→**Input:**
    53→- `token` (required): Token symbol (ETH, STRK, USDC, USDT) or contract address
    54→- `address` (optional): Wallet address (defaults to agent's address)
    55→
    56→**Response:**
    57→```json
    58→{
    59→  "address": "0x...",
    60→  "token": "ETH",
    61→  "tokenAddress": "REDACTED",
    62→  "balance": "1.5",
    63→  "raw": "1500000000000000000",
    64→  "decimals": 18
    65→}
    66→```
    67→
    68→#### starknet_get_balances (Multiple Tokens)
    69→
    70→Query balances for multiple tokens in a single RPC call. More efficient for portfolio views.
    71→
    72→**Input:**
    73→- `tokens` (required): Array of token symbols or addresses (max 200)
    74→- `address` (optional): Wallet address
    75→
    76→**Response:**
    77→```json
    78→{
    79→  "address": "0x...",
    80→  "balances": [
    81→    { "token": "ETH", "tokenAddress": "0x...", "balance": "1.5", "raw": "...", "decimals": 18 },
    82→    { "token": "USDC", "tokenAddress": "0x...", "balance": "100", "raw": "...", "decimals": 6 }
    83→  ],
    84→  "tokensQueried": 2,
    85→  "method": "balance_checker"
    86→}
    87→```
    88→
    89→**When to use:**
    90→- **starknet_get_balance**: Quick single-token check
    91→- **starknet_get_balances**: Portfolio overview, multi-token operations
    92→
    93→## Core Operations
    94→
    95→### Check Balance (Single Token)
    96→
    97→Use the `starknet_get_balance` MCP tool for simple single-token queries:
    98→
    99→```typescript
   100→// Via MCP tool (recommended)
   101→const result = await mcpClient.callTool({
   102→  name: "starknet_get_balance",
   103→  arguments: {
   104→    address: "0x...",  // Account address
   105→    token: "ETH",      // Symbol (ETH, STRK, USDC, USDT) or contract address
   106→  }
   107→});
   108→// Returns: { address, token, tokenAddress, balance, raw, decimals }
   109→```
   110→
   111→**Direct starknet.js usage:**
   112→
   113→```typescript
   114→import { RpcProvider, Contract } from "starknet";
   115→
   116→const provider = new RpcProvider({ nodeUrl: process.env.STARKNET_RPC_URL });
   117→
   118→// ETH balance (starknet.js v8 uses options object for Contract)
   119→const ethAddress = "REDACTED";
   120→const ethContract = new Contract({
   121→  abi: erc20Abi,
   122→  address: ethAddress,
   123→  providerOrAccount: provider,
   124→});
   125→const balance = await ethContract.balanceOf(accountAddress);
   126→
   127→// starknet.js v8: Convert uint256 to bigint
   128→const balanceBigInt = BigInt(balance.low) + (BigInt(balance.high) << 128n);
   129→// Format: (balanceBigInt / 10n ** 18n).toString() for whole units
   130→```
   131→
   132→### Check Multiple Balances (Batch)
   133→
   134→Use `starknet_get_balances` for efficient multi-token queries (single RPC call):
   135→
   136→```typescript
   137→// Via MCP tool (recommended for multiple tokens)
   138→const result = await mcpClient.callTool({
   139→  name: "starknet_get_balances",
   140→  arguments: {
   141→    address: "0x...",
   142→    tokens: ["ETH", "STRK", "USDC", "USDT"],  // Up to 200 tokens
   143→  }
   144→});
   145→
   146→// Returns:
   147→// {
   148→//   address: "0x...",
   149→//   balances: [
   150→//     { token: "ETH", tokenAddress: "0x...", balance: "1.5", raw: "1500000000000000000", decimals: 18 },
   151→//     { token: "STRK", tokenAddress: "0x...", balance: "100", raw: "100000000000000000000", decimals: 18 },
   152→//     ...
   153→//   ],
   154→//   tokensQueried: 4,
   155→//   method: "balance_checker"  // Uses BalanceChecker contract for efficiency
   156→// }
   157→```
   158→
   159→**Direct starknet.js usage with BalanceChecker contract:**
   160→
   161→```typescript
   162→import { RpcProvider, Contract } from "starknet";
   163→
   164→const provider = new RpcProvider({ nodeUrl: process.env.STARKNET_RPC_URL });
   165→
   166→// BalanceChecker contract (returns non-zero balances only)
   167→const BALANCE_CHECKER_ADDRESS = "0x031ce64a666fbf9a2b1b2ca51c2af60d9a76d3b85e5fbfb9d5a8dbd3fedc9716";
   168→const BALANCE_CHECKER_ABI = [
   169→  {
   170→    type: "struct",
   171→    name: "core::integer::u256",
   172→    members: [
   173→      { name: "low", type: "core::integer::u128" },
   174→      { name: "high", type: "core::integer::u128" },
   175→    ],
   176→  },
   177→  {
   178→    type: "struct",
   179→    name: "governance::balance_checker::NonZeroBalance",
   180→    members: [
   181→      { name: "token", type: "core::starknet::contract_address::ContractAddress" },
   182→      { name: "balance", type: "core::integer::u256" },
   183→    ],
   184→  },
   185→  {
   186→    type: "function",
   187→    name: "get_balances",
   188→    inputs: [
   189→      { name: "address", type: "core::starknet::contract_address::ContractAddress" },
   190→      { name: "tokens", type: "core::array::Span::<core::starknet::contract_address::ContractAddress>" },
   191→    ],
   192→    outputs: [{ type: "core::array::Span::<governance::balance_checker::NonZeroBalance>" }],
   193→    state_mutability: "view",
   194→  },
   195→];
   196→
   197→const balanceChecker = new Contract({
   198→  abi: BALANCE_CHECKER_ABI,
   199→  address: BALANCE_CHECKER_ADDRESS,
   200→  providerOrAccount: provider,
   201→});
   202→
   203→// Query multiple tokens at once
   204→const tokens = [
   205→  "REDACTED", // ETH
   206→  "REDACTED", // STRK
   207→  "0x053c91253bc9682c04929ca02ed00b3e423f6710d2ee7e0d5ebb06f3ecf368a8", // USDC
   208→];
   209→
   210→const result = await balanceChecker.get_balances(accountAddress, tokens);
   211→
   212→// Parse response (only non-zero balances returned)
   213→for (const item of result) {
   214→  const tokenAddr = "0x" + BigInt(item.token).toString(16).padStart(64, "0");
   215→  const balance = BigInt(item.balance); // starknet.js converts u256 to bigint
   216→  console.log(`${tokenAddr}: ${balance}`);
   217→}
   218→```
   219→
   220→**When to use which:**
   221→- `starknet_get_balance`: Single token, simple use case
   222→- `starknet_get_balances`: Multiple tokens, portfolio view, more efficient
   223→
   224→
   225→### Transfer Tokens
   226→
   227→Use the `starknet_transfer` MCP tool with optional gasless mode:
   228→
   229→```typescript
   230→// Via MCP tool (recommended)
   231→const result = await mcpClient.callTool({
   232→  name: "starknet_transfer",
   233→  arguments: {
   234→    recipient: "0x...",
   235→    token: "STRK",        // Symbol or contract address
   236→    amount: "10.5",       // Human-readable amount
   237→    gasfree: false,       // Optional: use paymaster
   238→  }
   239→});
   240→// Returns: { transactionHash, recipient, token, amount, gasfree }
   241→```
   242→
   243→**Gasless Transfer (Pay gas in token instead of ETH/STRK):**
   244→
   245→```typescript
   246→// Pay gas in USDC instead of ETH/STRK
   247→const result = await mcpClient.callTool({
   248→  name: "starknet_transfer",
   249→  arguments: {
   250→    recipient: "0x...",
   251→    token: "STRK",
   252→    amount: "100",
   253→    gasfree: true,
   254→    gasToken: "USDC",    // Gas paid in USDC
   255→  }
   256→});
   257→```
   258→
   259→**Direct starknet.js usage:**
   260→
   261→```typescript
   262→import { Account, RpcProvider, CallData, cairo, ETransactionVersion } from "starknet";
   263→
   264→const provider = new RpcProvider({ nodeUrl: process.env.STARKNET_RPC_URL });
   265→
   266→// starknet.js v8: Account uses options object
   267→const account = new Account({
   268→  provider,
   269→  address: process.env.STARKNET_ACCOUNT_ADDRESS,
   270→  signer: process.env.STARKNET_PRIVATE_KEY,
   271→  transactionVersion: ETransactionVersion.V3,
   272→});
   273→
   274→const tokenAddress = "REDACTED"; // STRK
   275→
   276→// starknet.js v8: Use cairo.uint256() instead of uint256.bnToUint256()
   277→const { transaction_hash } = await account.execute({
   278→  contractAddress: tokenAddress,
   279→  entrypoint: "transfer",
   280→  calldata: CallData.compile({
   281→    recipient: recipientAddress,
   282→    amount: cairo.uint256(amountInWei),
   283→  }),
   284→});
   285→await account.waitForTransaction(transaction_hash);
   286→```
   287→
   288→### Estimate Fees
   289→
   290→```typescript
   291→const estimatedFee = await account.estimateInvokeFee({
   292→  contractAddress: tokenAddress,
   293→  entrypoint: "transfer",
   294→  calldata: CallData.compile({
   295→    recipient: recipientAddress,
   296→    amount: cairo.uint256(amountInWei),
   297→  }),
   298→});
   299→// estimatedFee.overall_fee -- total fee in STRK (V3 transactions)
   300→```
   301→
   302→### Contract Interactions
   303→
   304→**Read contract state (view functions):**
   305→
   306→```typescript
   307→// Via MCP tool
   308→const result = await mcpClient.callTool({
   309→  name: "starknet_call_contract",
   310→  arguments: {
   311→    contractAddress: "0x...",
   312→    entrypoint: "balanceOf",
   313→    calldata: [accountAddress],
   314→  }
   315→});
   316→```
   317→
   318→**Write to contracts (state-changing functions):**
   319→
   320→```typescript
   321→// Via MCP tool with gasless option
   322→const result = await mcpClient.callTool({
   323→  name: "starknet_invoke_contract",
   324→  arguments: {
   325→    contractAddress: "0x...",
   326→    entrypoint: "approve",
   327→    calldata: [spenderAddress, ...uint256Amount],
   328→    gasfree: true,         // Optional: use paymaster
   329→    gasToken: "USDC",      // Optional: pay gas in token
   330→  }
   331→});
   332→```
   333→
   334→### Multi-Call (Batch Transactions)
   335→
   336→```typescript
   337→// Execute multiple operations in a single transaction
   338→const { transaction_hash } = await account.execute([
   339→  {
   340→    contractAddress: tokenA,
   341→    entrypoint: "approve",
   342→    calldata: CallData.compile({
   343→      spender: routerAddress,
   344→      amount: cairo.uint256(amount),
   345→    }),
   346→  },
   347→  {
   348→    contractAddress: routerAddress,
   349→    entrypoint: "swap",
   350→    calldata: CallData.compile({ /* swap params */ }),
   351→  },
   352→]);
   353→```
   354→
   355→### Gasless Transfer (Pay Gas in Token) - SDK v4 + PaymasterRpc
   356→
   357→```typescript
   358→import { getQuotes, executeSwap } from "@avnu/avnu-sdk";
   359→import { PaymasterRpc } from "starknet";
   360→
   361→// SDK v4: Use PaymasterRpc from starknet.js
   362→// Mainnet: https://starknet.paymaster.avnu.fi
   363→// Sepolia: https://sepolia.paymaster.avnu.fi
   364→const paymaster = new PaymasterRpc({
   365→  nodeUrl: process.env.AVNU_PAYMASTER_URL || "https://starknet.paymaster.avnu.fi",
   366→});
   367→
   368→// Any swap can be made gasless by adding paymaster option
   369→const result = await executeSwap({
   370→  provider: account,
   371→  quote: bestQuote,
   372→  slippage: 0.01,
   373→  executeApprove: true,
   374→  paymaster: {
   375→    active: true,
   376→    provider: paymaster,
   377→    params: {
   378→      feeMode: {
   379→        mode: "default",
   380→        gasToken: usdcAddress, // Pay gas in USDC instead of ETH/STRK
   381→      },
   382→    },
   383→  },
   384→});
   385→```
   386→
   387→## Token Resolution
   388→
   389→The MCP server uses TokenService to resolve token symbols and addresses. Static tokens (ETH, STRK, USDC, USDT) are always available. For other tokens, the service fetches metadata from avnu SDK.
   390→
   391→```typescript
   392→import { fetchTokenByAddress, fetchVerifiedTokenBySymbol } from '@avnu/avnu-sdk';
   393→
   394→// Get token by symbol (verified tokens only)
   395→const lords = await fetchVerifiedTokenBySymbol('LORDS');
   396→console.log(lords.address, lords.decimals); // 0x0124aeb..., 18
   397→
   398→// Get token by address (any token)
   399→const token = await fetchTokenByAddress('0x...');
   400→console.log(token.symbol, token.name, token.decimals);
   401→
   402→// Get all verified tokens
   403→import { fetchTokens } from '@avnu/avnu-sdk';
   404→const page = await fetchTokens({ tags: ['Verified'], size: 100 });
   405→page.content.forEach(t => console.log(t.symbol, t.address));
   406→```
   407→
   408→Static tokens available without network calls: ETH, STRK, USDC, USDT
   409→
   410→## Session Keys (Agent Autonomy)
   411→
   412→Session keys allow agents to execute pre-approved transactions without per-action human approval:
   413→
   414→1. Human owner creates a session key with policies:
   415→   - Allowed contract addresses and methods
   416→   - Maximum spending per transaction/period
   417→   - Expiry timestamp
   418→2. Agent uses the session key for autonomous operations
   419→3. Owner can revoke at any time
   420→
   421→Reference implementation: [Cartridge Controller](https://docs.cartridge.gg/controller/getting-started)
   422→
   423→## Configuration
   424→
   425→| Variable | Purpose | Default |
   426→|----------|---------|---------|
   427→| `STARKNET_RPC_URL` | Starknet JSON-RPC endpoint | Required |
   428→| `STARKNET_ACCOUNT_ADDRESS` | Agent's account address | Required |
   429→| `STARKNET_PRIVATE_KEY` | Agent's signing key | Required |
   430→| `AVNU_BASE_URL` | avnu API base URL | `https://starknet.api.avnu.fi` |
   431→| `AVNU_PAYMASTER_URL` | avnu paymaster URL | `https://starknet.paymaster.avnu.fi` |
   432→
   433→## Error Handling
   434→
   435→| Error | Cause | Resolution |
   436→|-------|-------|------------|
   437→| `INSUFFICIENT_BALANCE` | Not enough tokens | Check balance before transfer |
   438→| `INVALID_NONCE` | Nonce mismatch | Retry with fresh nonce |
   439→| `TRANSACTION_REVERTED` | Contract execution failed | Check calldata and allowances |
   440→| `FEE_TRANSFER_FAILURE` | Can't pay gas fee | Use paymaster or add ETH/STRK |
   441→
   442→## References
   443→
   444→- [starknet.js Documentation](https://www.starknetjs.com/)
   445→- [Starknet Account Abstraction](https://www.starknet.io/blog/native-account-abstraction/)
   446→- [Session Keys Guide](https://www.starknet.io/blog/session-keys-on-starknet-unlocking-gasless-secure-transactions/)
   447→- [avnu Paymaster](https://docs.avnu.fi/paymaster)
   448→

content.content:
1→---
     2→name: cairo-deploy
     3→description: Use when deploying Cairo contracts to Starknet — sncast commands, account setup, declare/deploy workflow, network configuration, contract verification.
     4→license: Apache-2.0
     5→metadata: {"author":"starknet-agentic","version":"1.0.0","org":"keep-starknet-strange"}
     6→keywords: [cairo, deploy, sncast, starknet, devnet, sepolia, mainnet, declare, verification]
     7→allowed-tools: [Bash, Read, Write, Glob, Grep, Task]
     8→user-invocable: true
     9→---
    10→
    11→# Cairo Deploy
    12→
    13→Reference for deploying Cairo smart contracts to Starknet using sncast (Starknet Foundry).
    14→
    15→## When to Use
    16→
    17→- Deploying contracts to Starknet devnet, Sepolia, or mainnet
    18→- Declaring contract classes
    19→- Setting up deployer accounts
    20→- Configuring network endpoints
    21→- Verifying deployed contracts
    22→- Invoking/calling deployed contracts
    23→
    24→**Not for:** Writing contracts (use cairo-contracts), testing (use cairo-testing), optimization (use cairo-optimization)
    25→
    26→## Setup
    27→
    28→### Install Starknet Foundry
    29→
    30→```bash
    31→# Install via asdf (recommended for version pinning)
    32→asdf plugin add starknet-foundry
    33→asdf install starknet-foundry 0.56.0
    34→asdf global starknet-foundry 0.56.0
    35→
    36→# Or install directly
    37→curl -L https://raw.githubusercontent.com/foundry-rs/starknet-foundry/master/scripts/install.sh | sh
    38→snfoundryup
    39→```
    40→
    41→### .tool-versions
    42→
    43→Pin versions for reproducible builds:
    44→
    45→```
    46→scarb 2.15.1
    47→starknet-foundry 0.56.0
    48→```
    49→
    50→> **Note:** snforge 0.56.0 requires Scarb >= 2.12.0. Check [github.com/foundry-rs/starknet-foundry/releases](https://github.com/foundry-rs/starknet-foundry/releases) for the latest.
    51→
    52→## Build
    53→
    54→```bash
    55→# Build contracts (generates Sierra + CASM)
    56→scarb build
    57→```
    58→
    59→Output goes to `target/dev/`:
    60→- `myproject_MyContract.contract_class.json` (Sierra)
    61→- `myproject_MyContract.compiled_contract_class.json` (CASM)
    62→
    63→## Account Setup
    64→
    65→### Create a New Account
    66→
    67→```bash
    68→# Generate account on Sepolia
    69→sncast account create \
    70→    --url https://starknet-sepolia.g.alchemy.com/v2/YOUR_KEY \
    71→    --name my-deployer
    72→
    73→# This outputs the account address — fund it with ETH/STRK before deploying
    74→
    75→# Deploy the account contract
    76→sncast account deploy \
    77→    --url https://starknet-sepolia.g.alchemy.com/v2/YOUR_KEY \
    78→    --name my-deployer
    79→```
    80→
    81→### Import Existing Account
    82→
    83→```bash
    84→sncast account add \
    85→    --url https://starknet-sepolia.g.alchemy.com/v2/YOUR_KEY \
    86→    --name my-deployer \
    87→    --address 0x123... \
    88→    --private-key 0xabc... \
    89→    --type oz
    90→```
    91→
    92→Account types: `oz` (OpenZeppelin), `argent`, `braavos`
    93→
    94→## sncast.toml
    95→
    96→Configure defaults to avoid repeating flags:
    97→
    98→```toml
    99→[default]
   100→url = "https://starknet-sepolia.g.alchemy.com/v2/YOUR_KEY"
   101→account = "my-deployer"
   102→accounts-file = "~/.starknet_accounts/starknet_open_zeppelin_accounts.json"
   103→wait = true
   104→
   105→[mainnet]
   106→url = "https://starknet-mainnet.g.alchemy.com/v2/YOUR_KEY"
   107→account = "mainnet-deployer"
   108→```
   109→
   110→Use profiles: `sncast --profile mainnet declare ...`
   111→
   112→## Declare (Register Class)
   113→
   114→Before deploying, declare the contract class on-chain:
   115→
   116→```bash
   117→# Declare contract
   118→sncast declare \
   119→    --contract-name MyContract
   120→
   121→# Output:
   122→# class_hash: 0x1234...
   123→# transaction_hash: 0xabcd...
   124→```
   125→
   126→If the class is already declared, sncast will tell you — that's fine, use the existing class hash.
   127→
   128→## Deploy (Create Instance)
   129→
   130→```bash
   131→# Deploy with constructor args
   132→sncast deploy \
   133→    --class-hash 0x1234... \
   134→    --constructor-calldata 0xOWNER_ADDRESS
   135→
   136→# Multiple constructor args (space-separated)
   137→sncast deploy \
   138→    --class-hash 0x1234... \
   139→    --constructor-calldata 0xOWNER 0xTOKEN_ADDRESS 1000
   140→```
   141→
   142→### Constructor Calldata Encoding
   143→
   144→Arguments are passed as felt252 values:
   145→- `ContractAddress` — pass as hex `0x123...`
   146→- `u256` — pass as TWO felts: `low high` (e.g., `1000 0` for 1000)
   147→- `felt252` — pass directly
   148→- `bool` — `1` for true, `0` for false
   149→- `ByteArray` (strings) — use sncast's string encoding or pass raw
   150→
   151→## Invoke (Write)
   152→
   153→```bash
   154→# Call a write function
   155→sncast invoke \
   156→    --contract-address 0xCONTRACT \
   157→    --function "transfer" \
   158→    --calldata 0xRECIPIENT 1000 0
   159→```
   160→
   161→## Call (Read)
   162→
   163→```bash
   164→# Call a view function (free, no tx)
   165→sncast call \
   166→    --contract-address 0xCONTRACT \
   167→    --function "get_balance" \
   168→    --calldata 0xACCOUNT
   169→```
   170→
   171→## Multicall
   172→
   173→Execute multiple calls in a single transaction:
   174→
   175→```bash
   176→# Create a multicall file
   177→cat > multicall.toml << 'EOF'
   178→[[call]]
   179→call_type = "deploy"
   180→class_hash = "0x1234..."
   181→inputs = ["0xOWNER"]
   182→
   183→[[call]]
   184→call_type = "invoke"
   185→contract_address = "0xTOKEN"
   186→function = "approve"
   187→inputs = ["0xSPENDER", "1000", "0"]
   188→EOF
   189→
   190→sncast multicall run --path multicall.toml
   191→```
   192→
   193→## Deploy Script Pattern
   194→
   195→For complex deployments, use a script:
   196→
   197→```bash
   198→#!/bin/bash
   199→set -euo pipefail
   200→
   201→RPC_URL="https://starknet-sepolia.g.alchemy.com/v2/YOUR_KEY"
   202→ACCOUNT="my-deployer"
   203→
   204→echo "Building..."
   205→scarb build
   206→
   207→echo "Declaring MyToken..."
   208→TOKEN_CLASS=$(sncast declare --contract-name MyToken --url $RPC_URL --account $ACCOUNT | grep "class_hash" | awk '{print $2}')
   209→echo "Token class: $TOKEN_CLASS"
   210→
   211→echo "Deploying MyToken..."
   212→TOKEN_ADDR=$(sncast deploy --class-hash $TOKEN_CLASS --constructor-calldata 0xOWNER --url $RPC_URL --account $ACCOUNT | grep "contract_address" | awk '{print $2}')
   213→echo "Token deployed at: $TOKEN_ADDR"
   214→
   215→echo "Declaring AMM..."
   216→AMM_CLASS=$(sncast declare --contract-name AMM --url $RPC_URL --account $ACCOUNT | grep "class_hash" | awk '{print $2}')
   217→
   218→echo "Deploying AMM..."
   219→AMM_ADDR=$(sncast deploy --class-hash $AMM_CLASS --constructor-calldata $TOKEN_ADDR --url $RPC_URL --account $ACCOUNT | grep "contract_address" | awk '{print $2}')
   220→echo "AMM deployed at: $AMM_ADDR"
   221→
   222→echo "Done. Addresses:"
   223→echo "  Token: $TOKEN_ADDR"
   224→echo "  AMM:   $AMM_ADDR"
   225→```
   226→
   227→## Network Endpoints
   228→
   229→| Network | RPC URL |
   230→|---------|---------|
   231→| Devnet (local) | `http://localhost:5050` |
   232→| Sepolia (testnet) | `https://starknet-sepolia.g.alchemy.com/v2/KEY` |
   233→| Mainnet | `https://starknet-mainnet.g.alchemy.com/v2/KEY` |
   234→
   235→Alternative providers: Infura, Blast, Nethermind (free tier available).
   236→
   237→### Local Devnet
   238→
   239→```bash
   240→# Install and run starknet-devnet-rs
   241→cargo install starknet-devnet
   242→starknet-devnet --seed 42
   243→
   244→# Devnet provides pre-funded accounts — use them for testing
   245→```
   246→
   247→## Contract Verification
   248→
   249→Verify source code on Voyager or Starkscan:
   250→
   251→```bash
   252→# Verify on Voyager (manual: upload Sierra JSON via web UI)
   253→# https://sepolia.voyager.online/contract/0xADDRESS#code
   254→
   255→# Or use Walnut for programmatic verification
   256→# https://app.walnut.dev
   257→```
   258→
   259→> **Note:** `sncast verify` currently only supports the Walnut verification backend. Voyager and Starkscan verification must be done through their respective web UIs.
   260→
   261→## Upgradeable Contracts
   262→
   263→For contracts using OZ UpgradeableComponent:
   264→
   265→```bash
   266→# 1. Declare new class
   267→sncast declare --contract-name MyContractV2
   268→
   269→# 2. Call upgrade on existing contract
   270→sncast invoke \
   271→    --contract-address 0xEXISTING_CONTRACT \
   272→    --function "upgrade" \
   273→    --calldata 0xNEW_CLASS_HASH
   274→```
   275→
   276→## Common Errors
   277→
   278→| Error | Cause | Fix |
   279→|-------|-------|-----|
   280→| `Contract not found` | Account not deployed | Run `sncast account deploy` |
   281→| `Insufficient max fee` | Not enough ETH/STRK for gas | Fund the deployer account |
   282→| `Class already declared` | Same class hash exists | Use the existing class hash for deploy |
   283→| `Entry point not found` | Wrong function name | Check the contract ABI |
   284→| `Invalid calldata` | Wrong number/type of args | Check constructor signature, remember u256 = 2 felts |
   285→

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

1→---
     2→name: starknet-anonymous-wallet
     3→description: Create an anonymous Starknet wallet via Typhoon and interact with Starknet contracts. Privacy-focused wallet creation for agents requiring anonymity.
     4→license: Apache-2.0
     5→metadata: {"author":"starknet-agentic","version":"1.0.0","org":"keep-starknet-strange"}
     6→keywords: [starknet, wallet, anonymous, transfer, balance, anonymous-agent-wallet, strk, eth, privacy, typhoon]
     7→allowed-tools: [Bash, Read, Write, Glob, Grep, Task]
     8→user-invocable: true
     9→---
    10→
    11→# starknet-anonymous-wallet
    12→
    13→This skill provides **agent-facing scripts** for:
    14→- Creating/loading a Starknet account (Typhoon flow)
    15→- Discovering ABI / functions
    16→- Reading & writing to contracts
    17→- Preflight (simulate + fee estimate)
    18→- Allowance checks with human amounts
    19→
    20→## Prerequisites
    21→
    22→```bash
    23→npm install starknet@^9.2.1 typhoon-sdk@^1.1.13
    24→```
    25→
    26→### RPC setup (required for onchain reads/writes)
    27→
    28→These scripts talk to Starknet via JSON-RPC. Configure one of:
    29→
    30→- Set `STARKNET_RPC_URL` in your environment (recommended), OR
    31→- Pass `rpcUrl` in the JSON input for scripts that support it.
    32→
    33→If neither is provided, scripts fall back to the public Lava mainnet RPC:
    34→- `https://rpc.starknet.lava.build:443`
    35→
    36→## CRITICAL: Account Creation Flow
    37→
    38→When the user asks to create a anonymous Starknet account (in any form like "create an anonymous account", "create a Starknet anonymous account for my agent", "I need a anonymous wallet", etc.), **ALWAYS follow this flow**:
    39→
    40→### Step 1: Check if account already exists
    41→
    42→```bash
    43→node scripts/check-account.js
    44→```
    45→
    46→**If `hasAccount: false`:**
    47→- Proceed to Step 2
    48→
    49→### Step 2: Provide funding instructions
    50→
    51→Tell the user:
    52→
    53→---
    54→
    55→**To create your Starknet account, you need to fund it through Typhoon:**
    56→
    57→1. Go to the Typhoon website: https://www.typhoon-finance.com/app
    58→2. Make a deposit and download your deposit note
    59→   - **Recommended:** Make a STRK deposit (this will be used to deploy and fund your agent account)
    60→3. Copy **all the content** of your downloaded note file and paste it here
    61→
    62→---
    63→
    64→Then **wait for the user to paste the note content**.
    65→
    66→### Step 3: Create the account
    67→
    68→> Note: **Account creation can take a few minutes**. Typhoon proof generation + Starknet deployment/finality are not instant; tell the user to wait and avoid retrying unless it fails.
    69→
    70→Once the user pastes the note JSON, run:
    71→
    72→```bash
    73→node scripts/create-account.js '<paste the note JSON here>'
    74→```
    75→
    76→The note format is:
    77→```json
    78→{
    79→  "secret": "0x...",
    80→  "nullifier": "0x...",
    81→  "txHash": "0x...",
    82→  "pool": "0x...",
    83→  "day": "0x..."
    84→}
    85→```
    86→
    87→### Step 4: Confirm success
    88→
    89→After successful creation, show the user:
    90→- Their new account address
    91→- Explorer link (Voyager/Starkscan)
    92→- Remind them the private key is stored securely
    93→
    94→---
    95→
    96→## Show Account Address
    97→
    98→When user asks "what's my address", "show my wallet", "my account address", etc.:
    99→
   100→```bash
   101→node scripts/show-address.js
   102→```
   103→
   104→If multiple accounts exist, it returns all. Pass index to get specific one:
   105→```bash
   106→node scripts/show-address.js 0
   107→```
   108→
   109→---
   110→
   111→## Scripts Reference
   112→
   113→| Script | Purpose |
   114→|--------|---------|
   115→| `check-account.js` | Check if account(s) exist |
   116→| `show-address.js` | Show account address(es) |
   117→| `load-account.js` | Load an existing local account artifact |
   118→| `create-account.js` | Create + deploy a new account via Typhoon |
   119→| `get-abi.js` | Fetch ABI summary + list functions (+ optional candidate ranking) |
   120→| `call-contract.js` | Call a view function |
   121→| `invoke-contract.js` | Call an external function |
   122→| `check-allowance.js` | Check ERC20 allowance (supports human amount) |
   123→| `multicall.js` | Execute multiple calls in one tx |
   124→| `estimate-fee.js` | Preflight fee estimate for a call/multicall |
   125→| `simulate.js` | Preflight simulate for a call/multicall |
   126→| `token-info.js` | Token metadata (decodes felt short strings) |
   127→| `decode-felt.js` | Decode felt short strings |
   128→| `sign-typed-data.js` | Sign typedData (for SIWS / Starkbook-style auth) |
   129→| `sign-invoke-tx.js` | Sign an INVOKE transaction (one or more calls) without broadcasting |
   130→
   131→---
   132→## Core Agent Workflow (no hardcoding)
   133→
   134→### 1) Address & docs discovery (agent planning)
   135→If the user mentions protocols/tokens/apps (e.g. "Ekubo", "STRK", "ETH"), the **agent must first search** for:
   136→- The relevant contract addresses
   137→- The protocol documentation
   138→
   139→**Research constraint:** all agent research must be done through **MCP** (Model Context Protocol) — no interactive browser/UI. Use machine-readable sources (APIs, docs URLs, GitHub raw files) via agent fetch tools.
   140→
   141→This skill does **not** do web search by itself; it provides the onchain tooling once addresses are known.
   142→
   143→### 2) Load account
   144→```bash
   145→node scripts/load-account.js
   146→```
   147→
   148→### 3) ABI discovery (+ optional ranking)
   149→```bash
   150→node scripts/get-abi.js '{"contractAddress":"0x..."}'
   151→```
   152→
   153→If you want the script to return **ranked candidates** (to help the agent decide), pass a `query`:
   154→```bash
   155→node scripts/get-abi.js '{"contractAddress":"0x...","query":"swap exact tokens for tokens"}'
   156→```
   157→
   158→### 4) Read
   159→```bash
   160→node scripts/call-contract.js '{"contractAddress":"0x...","method":"<view_fn>","args":[...]}'
   161→```
   162→
   163→Optional: decode felt short strings:
   164→```bash
   165→node scripts/call-contract.js '{"contractAddress":"0x...","method":"symbol","args":[],"decodeShortStrings":true}'
   166→```
   167→
   168→### 5) Allowance check (raw or human)
   169→Raw base units:
   170→```bash
   171→node scripts/check-allowance.js '{"tokenAddress":"0x...","ownerAddress":"0x...","spenderAddress":"0x...","requiredAmount":"20000000000000000000"}'
   172→```
   173→Human amount (script fetches decimals):
   174→```bash
   175→node scripts/check-allowance.js '{"tokenAddress":"0x...","ownerAddress":"0x...","spenderAddress":"0x...","requiredAmountHuman":"20"}'
   176→```
   177→
   178→### 6) Preflight (recommended)
   179→Fee estimate:
   180→```bash
   181→node scripts/estimate-fee.js '{"privateKeyPath":"...","accountAddress":"0x...","calls":[{"contractAddress":"0x...","method":"...","args":[...]}]}'
   182→```
   183→Simulation:
   184→```bash
   185→node scripts/simulate.js '{"privateKeyPath":"...","accountAddress":"0x...","calls":[{"contractAddress":"0x...","method":"...","args":[...]}]}'
   186→```
   187→
   188→### 7) Execute
   189→Single write:
   190→```bash
   191→node scripts/invoke-contract.js '{"privateKeyPath":"...","accountAddress":"0x...","contractAddress":"0x...","method":"...","args":[...]}'
   192→```
   193→
   194→---
   195→
   196→## Sign typedData (for Starkbook / SIWS)
   197→
   198→When you need a Starknet account to sign a SIWS challenge (typedData) **without ever exposing the private key**, use:
   199→
   200→```bash
   201→node scripts/sign-typed-data.js '{
   202→  "accountAddress":"0x...",
   203→  "typedData": { "domain": { }, "types": { }, "primaryType": "Message", "message": { } }
   204→}'
   205→```
   206→
   207→Or if you saved the typedData to a file:
   208→
   209→```bash
   210→node scripts/sign-typed-data.js '{
   211→  "accountAddress":"0x...",
   212→  "typedDataPath":"/tmp/typedData.json"
   213→}'
   214→```
   215→
   216→Output is a signature array (hex strings) that can be submitted to verification endpoints (e.g. Starkbook `/api/auth/verify`).
   217→
   218→---
   219→
   220→## Sign an INVOKE transaction (no broadcast)
   221→
   222→To sign a transaction **without sending it**, use:
   223→
   224→```bash
   225→node scripts/sign-invoke-tx.js '{
   226→  "accountAddress":"0x...",
   227→  "calls":[
   228→    {"contractAddress":"0xTOKEN","entrypoint":"transfer","calldata":["0xTO","<uint256_low>","<uint256_high>"]}
   229→  ]
   230→}'
   231→```
   232→
   233→Or with ABI args (the script will fetch ABI and compile calldata for you):
   234→
   235→```bash
   236→node scripts/sign-invoke-tx.js '{
   237→  "accountAddress":"0x...",
   238→  "calls":[
   239→    {"contractAddress":"0xTOKEN","method":"transfer","args":["0xTO","123"]}
   240→  ]
   241→}'
   242→```
   243→
   244→This returns an `invokeTransaction` payload suitable for RPC `starknet_addInvokeTransaction` (signature included) plus a fee estimate.
   245→
   246→⚠️ Not broadcast: this script only signs. To actually send, you must submit the payload to an RPC endpoint (and you should confirm before broadcasting).
   247→
   248→### Starkbook end-to-end helper (recommended)
   249→If you want a single command that does challenge → sign locally → verify → (optional) post **without Starkbook ever touching a private key**:
   250→
   251→```bash
   252→node scripts/starkbook-client.js '{
   253→  "base":"http://localhost:3000",
   254→  "accountAddress":"0x...",
   255→  "action":"post",
   256→  "body":"hello from agent",
   257→  "linkUrl":"https://example.com"
   258→}'
   259→```
   260→
   261→Approve + action in one tx:
   262→```bash
   263→node scripts/multicall.js '{"privateKeyPath":"...","accountAddress":"0x...","calls":[{"contractAddress":"0x...","method":"approve","args":["0xspender","123"]},{"contractAddress":"0x...","method":"...","args":[...]}]}'
   264→```
   265→

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

1→---
     2→name: controller-cli
     3→description: "Use when installing and operating the Cartridge Controller CLI (`controller`) to create human-approved sessions and execute Starknet transactions (JSON-only, explicit network selection, least-privilege policy scoping, paymaster control, and error recovery)."
     4→license: Apache-2.0
     5→metadata:
     6→  author: keep-starknet-strange
     7→  version: "1.0.0"
     8→  org: keep-starknet-strange
     9→compatibility: "controller-cli >=0.1.x, Python 3.8+"
    10→keywords:
    11→  - starknet
    12→  - cartridge
    13→  - controller
    14→  - controller-cli
    15→  - session
    16→  - paymaster
    17→  - voyager
    18→allowed-tools:
    19→  - Bash
    20→  - Read
    21→  - Write
    22→  - Grep
    23→user-invocable: true
    24→---
    25→
    26→# Cartridge Controller CLI
    27→
    28→Use this skill when you need to run the Cartridge Controller CLI (`controller-cli`) to create a scoped session (human-approved) and then execute Starknet transactions via that session.
    29→
    30→## Non-Negotiable Rules
    31→
    32→- Always pass `--json` and treat outputs as JSON.
    33→- Always be explicit about network. Never rely on defaults:
    34→  - `--chain-id SN_MAIN|SN_SEPOLIA`, or
    35→  - `--rpc-url <explicit url>`.
    36→- `controller register` requires human browser authorization. Do not automate/bypass it.
    37→- Use least-privilege policies only. Do not add token transfer permissions unless explicitly requested.
    38→- Transaction links: use Voyager only:
    39→  - Mainnet: `https://voyager.online/tx/0x...`
    40→  - Sepolia: `https://sepolia.voyager.online/tx/0x...`
    41→
    42→## Quick Start (Install)
    43→
    44→```bash
    45→curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/cartridge-gg/controller-cli/main/install.sh | bash
    46→export PATH="$PATH:$HOME/.local/bin"
    47→controller --version
    48→```
    49→
    50→## Safer Wrapper (Recommended)
    51→
    52→This skill includes a small wrapper that enforces the rules above and normalizes output:
    53→
    54→```bash
    55→python3 scripts/controller_safe.py status
    56→python3 scripts/controller_safe.py register --preset loot-survivor --chain-id SN_MAIN
    57→python3 scripts/controller_safe.py execute 0xCONTRACT entrypoint 0xCALLDATA --rpc-url https://api.cartridge.gg/x/starknet/sepolia
    58→```
    59→
    60→Behavior:
    61→- Adds `--json` if missing.
    62→- Refuses to run `call|execute|register|transaction` without `--chain-id` or `--rpc-url`.
    63→- Parses stdout as JSON.
    64→- If `.status == "error"`, prints `error_code`, `message`, `recovery_hint` and exits non-zero.
    65→
    66→## Deterministic Workflow
    67→
    68→### 1) Generate Keypair
    69→
    70→```bash
    71→controller generate --json
    72→```
    73→
    74→The private key is stored locally (typically `~/.config/controller-cli/`).
    75→
    76→### 2) Check Session Status
    77→
    78→```bash
    79→controller status --json
    80→```
    81→
    82→Common states:
    83→- `no_session` (no keypair)
    84→- `keypair_only` (needs registration)
    85→- `active` (registered and not expired)
    86→
    87→### 3) Register Session (Human Approval Required)
    88→
    89→Requirement: a human must approve in a browser.
    90→
    91→Option A (preferred): preset policies:
    92→
    93→```bash
    94→controller register --preset loot-survivor --chain-id SN_MAIN --json
    95→```
    96→
    97→Option B: least-privilege policy file:
    98→
    99→```bash
   100→controller register --file policy.json --rpc-url https://api.cartridge.gg/x/starknet/sepolia --json
   101→```
   102→
   103→Authorization output includes `short_url` and/or `authorization_url`:
   104→- Display `short_url` if present; otherwise display `authorization_url`.
   105→- Ask the user to open it and approve.
   106→- The CLI blocks until approved or timeout (typically ~6 minutes).
   107→
   108→### 4) Execute Transaction
   109→
   110→Single call (positional args: contract, entrypoint, calldata):
   111→
   112→```bash
   113→controller execute 0xCONTRACT transfer 0xRECIPIENT,0xAMOUNT_LOW,0xAMOUNT_HIGH \
   114→  --rpc-url https://api.cartridge.gg/x/starknet/sepolia \
   115→  --json
   116→```
   117→
   118→Multiple calls from file:
   119→
   120→```bash
   121→controller execute --file calls.json --rpc-url https://api.cartridge.gg/x/starknet/sepolia --json
   122→```
   123→
   124→Optional confirmation wait:
   125→
   126→```bash
   127→controller execute --file calls.json --rpc-url https://api.cartridge.gg/x/starknet/sepolia --wait --timeout 300 --json
   128→```
   129→
   130→### 5) Read-Only Call (No Session Needed)
   131→
   132→```bash
   133→controller call 0xCONTRACT balance_of 0xADDRESS --chain-id SN_SEPOLIA --json
   134→```
   135→
   136→### 6) Transaction Status
   137→
   138→```bash
   139→controller transaction 0xTX_HASH --chain-id SN_SEPOLIA --wait --timeout 300 --json
   140→```
   141→
   142→### 7) Lookup Usernames / Addresses
   143→
   144→```bash
   145→controller lookup --usernames alice,bob --json
   146→controller lookup --addresses 0x123...,0x456... --json
   147→```
   148→
   149→## Network Selection
   150→
   151→Always be explicit about network.
   152→
   153→Supported networks:
   154→
   155→| Chain ID | RPC URL |
   156→| --- | --- |
   157→| `SN_MAIN` | `https://api.cartridge.gg/x/starknet/mainnet` |
   158→| `SN_SEPOLIA` | `https://api.cartridge.gg/x/starknet/sepolia` |
   159→
   160→Priority order:
   161→1. `--rpc-url` flag
   162→2. Stored session RPC URL (from registration)
   163→3. Config default (lowest)
   164→
   165→If network is ambiguous:
   166→1. Run `controller status --json`
   167→2. Match the session `chain_id`, or ask the user
   168→
   169→## Paymaster Control
   170→
   171→Default behavior uses the paymaster (free execution). If the paymaster is unavailable, the transaction fails (no silent fallback).
   172→
   173→To self-pay with user funds:
   174→
   175→```bash
   176→controller execute ... --no-paymaster --json
   177→```
   178→
   179→## Amount Encoding (u256)
   180→
   181→Many ERC20-style amounts are `u256` split into `(low, high)` u128 limbs.
   182→
   183→For values that fit in u128 (most cases): set `high = 0x0`.
   184→
   185→Example calldata:
   186→`0xRECIPIENT,0x64,0x0`
   187→
   188→## Error Handling
   189→
   190→Errors are JSON:
   191→
   192→```json
   193→{
   194→  "status": "error",
   195→  "error_code": "ErrorType",
   196→  "message": "...",
   197→  "recovery_hint": "..."
   198→}
   199→```
   200→
   201→Always branch on `error_code` and follow `recovery_hint`.
   202→
   203→Common recoveries:
   204→- `NoSession`: run `controller generate --json`
   205→- `SessionExpired`: re-run `controller register ... --json`
   206→- `ManualExecutionRequired`: policy does not authorize the call; tighten/adjust policy and re-register
   207→- `CallbackTimeout`: user did not approve quickly enough; re-run `register` and retry
   208→
   209→## Input Validation
   210→
   211→Addresses must be `0x`-prefixed hex.
   212→
   213→```bash
   214→python3 scripts/validate_hex_address.py 0xabc...
   215→```
   216→

But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
